title: GPT-5.4 Thinking in ChatGPT
content:
GPT‑5.4 brings together the best of our recent advances in reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows into a single frontier model. It incorporates the industry-leading coding capabilities ofGPT‑5.3‑Codex⁠while improving how the model works across tools, software environments, and professional tasks involving spreadsheets, presentations, and documents. The result is a model that gets complex real work done accurately, effectively, and efficiently—delivering what you asked for with less back and forth.
In ChatGPT, GPT‑5.4 Thinking can now provide an upfront plan of its thinking, so you canadjust course mid-responsewhile it’s working,and arrive at a final output that’s more closely aligned with what you need without additional turns. GPT‑5.4 Thinking also improvesdeep web research,particularly for highly specific queries, whilebetter maintaining contextfor questions that require longer thinking. Together, these improvements mean higher-quality answers that arrive faster and stay relevant to the task at hand.
title_updated: GPT-5.4 Thinking: Launch frontier model combining reasoning, coding & agentic workflows
content_updated:
OpenAI releases GPT-5.4 Thinking, a frontier model that unifies advances in reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows into a single system, incorporating the coding capabilities of GPT-5.3-Codex while improving performance across tools, spreadsheets, presentations, and documents.
In ChatGPT, GPT-5.4 Thinking now provides an upfront plan of its reasoning so users can adjust course mid-response, improving deep web research for specific queries and maintaining better context during extended thinking sessions.
GPT-5.4 builds on the GPT-5 series' iterative improvement pattern, succeeding GPT-5.3 as the model that gets complex real-world work done with less back-and-forth interaction required from the user.

title: Codex app on Windows
content: The Codex app is now available on Windows for ChatGPT plans that include Codex. The app gives users a Windows desktop surface for running multiple Codex agents in parallel, with isolated worktrees and reviewable diffs that can be edited, discarded, or turned into a pull request.Users can sign in with ChatGPT from the app and keep work moving across the app, CLI, and IDE without switching between tools for every task. Learn more:Using Codex with your ChatGPT plan.
title_updated: Codex App: Launch Windows desktop client for parallel coding agents
content_updated:
The Codex app is now available on Windows for ChatGPT plans that include Codex, providing a desktop surface for running multiple Codex agents in parallel with isolated worktrees and reviewable diffs.
Users can sign in with their ChatGPT account and manage tasks across the app, CLI, and IDE without switching tools, with the ability to edit, discard, or turn diffs into pull requests directly.
This expands Codex's platform availability beyond macOS, which launched in February 2026, bringing the same parallel-agent workflow to Windows users.

title: GPT-5.3 Instant Update
content: GPT‑5.3 Instant delivers more accurate answers, richer and better-contextualized results when searching the web, and reduces unnecessary dead ends, caveats, and overly declarative phrasing that can interrupt the flow of conversation.This update focuses on the parts of the ChatGPT experience people feel every day: tone, relevance, and conversational flow. These are nuanced problems that don’t always show up in benchmarks, but shape whether ChatGPT feels helpful or frustrating. GPT‑5.3 Instant directly reflects user feedback in these areas.
title_updated: GPT-5.3 Instant: Improve accuracy, web search & conversational flow
content_updated:
GPT-5.3 Instant delivers more accurate answers, richer web search results with better contextualization, and reduces unnecessary dead ends, caveats, and overly declarative phrasing that interrupt conversational flow.
This update focuses on everyday ChatGPT experience qualities like tone, relevance, and conversational flow, addressing nuanced issues that do not always appear in benchmarks but significantly shape whether the tool feels helpful.
GPT-5.3 Instant directly reflects accumulated user feedback, continuing the iterative improvement cadence within the GPT-5 model generation.

title: ChatGPT Web and Android updates: Edit image prompts, share faster, and smoother conversations
content:
Web: Edit messages with images.You can now edit messages that include image attachments, so you don’t have to start over to adjust your prompt or regenerate results.
Web: Open search results in a new tab.Cmd+click in the search panel now opens results in a new tab, making it easier to explore without losing your place.
Web: Faster sharing.Sharing chats is now easier and faster, with a quicker-loading share menu.
Web: Export visuals from Code Blocks.Flowcharts, diagrams, and data visualizations created in Code Blocks can now be exported as images for easy reuse.
Android: Automatic scroll to latest message.Existing conversations now automatically scroll to the bottom, so you can jump right back in.
Android: Faster sidebar actions.Sidebar interactions, such as renaming chats, are now faster and more responsive.
Android: Improved dictation visuals.Dictation now has a clearer, more streamlined interface that keeps your text visible and updates as you speak.
Android: Keyboard opens automatically when attaching files.The keyboard now opens automatically after you attach a file, helping you continue your message seamlessly.
Bug fixes: Fixed increased message streaming errors in GPT-5.2 Thinking
Bug fixes: Fixed a flicker affecting the Voice Mode button when opening a conversation
Bug fixes: Sidebar actions on mobile web no longer stick while scrolling
title_updated: ChatGPT Web & Android: Add image prompt editing, faster sharing & more
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces multiple updates across Web and Android, including the ability to edit messages with image attachments on Web, opening search results in new tabs, faster sharing, and exportable Code Block visuals like flowcharts and diagrams.
Android improvements include automatic scrolling to the latest message, faster sidebar actions, improved dictation visuals, and automatic keyboard opening after file attachment, alongside bug fixes for GPT-5.2 Thinking streaming errors and Voice Mode button flickering.
This is a bundled release addressing everyday usability across both platforms, with Web receiving four feature updates and Android receiving four UX enhancements plus three bug fixes.

title: Add sources to your projects from anywhere
content:
Projects now make it easy to build a living knowledge base by adding sources wherever your context already lives; from apps, conversations, or quick text inputs.
Sources from apps:Paste a link to a Slack channel or Google Drive file or folder to add it directly as a project source.Additionally, use connected apps directly in chats.
Sources from chats:Save useful ChatGPT responses into your project so great outputs become reusable knowledge.
Ad-hoc text sources:Paste notes, briefs, or reference material directly to capture context instantly.
Learn more aboutprojects in ChatGPT.
title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Add sources from apps, chats & ad-hoc text
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects now lets users build a living knowledge base by adding sources from connected apps like Slack and Google Drive, saving useful ChatGPT responses from chats, and pasting ad-hoc text such as notes, briefs, or reference material.
Users can paste links to Slack channels or Google Drive files to add them directly as project sources, and connected apps can be used directly in chats for seamless context integration.
This update transforms Projects from a static file repository into a dynamic knowledge base that captures context from wherever it already lives, reducing the friction of manually uploading documents.

title: Expanded context window for Thinking
content: ChatGPT now has a total context window length of 256k tokens (128k input and 128k max output) when you manually select Thinking. Previously it was 196k total tokens.
title_updated: ChatGPT Thinking: Expand context window to 256k tokens (128k in, 128k out)
content_updated:
ChatGPT now supports a total context window of 256,000 tokens when Thinking mode is manually selected, split evenly between 128,000 input tokens and 128,000 maximum output tokens.
This represents a significant increase from the previous 196,000 total tokens, giving users more room for both complex inputs and extended model reasoning in a single session.
The expanded context is available when users manually select Thinking mode, enabling longer and more detailed analyses for complex tasks that require extended reasoning chains.

title: Interactive Code Blocks in ChatGPT
content:
We improved Code Blocks in ChatGPT to make them more interactive.You can write, edit, and preview your code in ChatGPT, all in oneplace:
Write and edit text in-line
Preview diagrams and mini apps directly in chat
Review code in split-screen views
title_updated: Code Blocks: Launch interactive write, edit & preview in ChatGPT
content_updated:
ChatGPT Code Blocks have been upgraded to be fully interactive, allowing users to write, edit, and preview code all in one place within the chat interface.
Users can now write and edit text in-line, preview diagrams and mini apps directly in chat, and review code in split-screen views without leaving the conversation.
This update transforms Code Blocks from a read-only display into an interactive development environment, reducing the need to switch between ChatGPT and external editors for quick coding tasks.

title: Retiring GPT-4o and other legacy models
content: Aspreviously announced, we have retired GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and OpenAI o4-mini from ChatGPT. We are also retiring GPT-5 (Instant and Thinking),as previously announced. There are no API changes at this time. For details, see ourblog postandHelp Center.
title_updated: Legacy Models: Execute retirement of GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, o4-mini & GPT-5 Instant/Thinking
content_updated:
OpenAI has retired GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, OpenAI o4-mini, GPT-5 Instant, and GPT-5 Thinking from ChatGPT as previously announced, completing the legacy model cleanup.
API access to these models remains unchanged; the retirement applies only to the ChatGPT consumer product, with users having been migrated to GPT-5.2 as the successor model.
This follows the January 29 advance notice and completes the transition announced two weeks prior, consolidating ChatGPT around the GPT-5.2 model family.

title: ChatGPT updates: Better file uploads, easier copying, and more reliable long chats
content:
Web: Attach up to 20 files in a single message.You can now upload up to 20 files at once (previously 10), making it easier to analyze document sets, compare multiple files, or provide broader context in a single message.
Web: Expanded file type support.We now support additional text and code file formats, improving compatibility with source files, logs, configuration files, and other structured text.
Web: Cleaner “Select All” behavior (Cmd+A).Cmd+A now selects only the conversation content, excluding surrounding interface elements for cleaner copying and sharing of chat transcripts.
Android: Quick tools in the composer.A new quick-tools menu in the message composer makes common actions easier to access.
Android: Simplified composer.The message composer has been streamlined to reduce visual complexity and make it easier to focus on your message.
iOS: Performance improvements.We’ve made multiple performance enhancements to improve responsiveness and overall speed throughout the app.
Web, iOS, and Android: Improved reliability for long “Thinking” chats.We've reduced interruption rates in longer “Thinking” chats through infrastructure improvements.
title_updated: ChatGPT Updates: Expand file uploads to 20, add file types & improve long chats
content_updated:
ChatGPT now supports uploading up to 20 files in a single message on Web, up from 10, with expanded file type support for text, code, source files, logs, and configuration files.
Additional improvements include cleaner Cmd+A selection behavior on Web, quick tools in the Android composer, a streamlined Android message composer, iOS performance enhancements, and improved reliability for long Thinking chats across all platforms.
This bundled release addresses productivity across Web, iOS, and Android, with the file upload expansion and new format support being the most impactful changes for users working with multiple documents.

title: ChatGPT Voice Update
content: We’re making an update to ChatGPT Voice that improves its ability to follow user instructions, and usage of tools like web search to provide better responses. This update applies to the version of Voice primarily used by those on the free ChatGPT plan, and by Plus users when limits are reached for the main model.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Improve instruction following & web search for free-tier users
content_updated:
ChatGPT Voice receives an update that improves its ability to follow user instructions and use tools like web search to provide better responses for free-tier and rate-limited Plus users.
This update targets the standard Voice mode used primarily by free ChatGPT users and by Plus users when limits are reached for the main model, rather than the Advanced Voice mode.
The improvement continues OpenAI's pattern of incremental Voice quality updates, following similar instruction-following fixes deployed for paid users in January 2026.

title: Updates to deep research
content:
We’re introducing improvements to deep research in ChatGPT to help produce more accurate, credible reports with greater control. You can now focus research on specific websites and a larger collection of connected apps as trusted sources.A redesigned sidebar entry point and fullscreen report view make it easier to start, review, and manage research in one place. Create and edit a research plan before it begins, and track progress with the ability to adjust direction mid-run.
Rolling out throughout today to Plus and Pro users, with Free and Go users following in the coming days.For more information, please refer to our help center article:Deep research in ChatGPT
title_updated: Deep Research: Launch editable research plans, focused sources & fullscreen reports
content_updated:
ChatGPT Deep Research introduces improvements for more accurate and credible reports with greater user control, including the ability to focus research on specific websites and connected apps as trusted sources.
A redesigned sidebar entry point and fullscreen report view make it easier to start, review, and manage research, with the ability to create and edit a research plan before it begins and adjust direction mid-run.
Rolling out to Plus and Pro users first, with Free and Go users following in coming days, this update represents a significant step toward user-directed research with real-time course correction.

title: GPT-5.2 Instant Update
content: We’re making an update to GPT-5.2 Instant in ChatGPT and the API that improves response style and quality.Users should notice responses that are more measured and grounded in tone, in a way that’s more contextually appropriate to the conversation. The model also tends to output clearer, more relevant answers to advice-seeking and how-to questions, more reliably placing the most important info upfront.
title_updated: GPT-5.2 Instant: Improve response style, tone & answer relevance
content_updated:
GPT-5.2 Instant receives an update in ChatGPT and the API that improves response style and quality, delivering more measured and grounded tone that is contextually appropriate to conversations.
The model now produces clearer, more relevant answers to advice-seeking and how-to questions, more reliably placing the most important information upfront in responses.
This continues the iterative refinement of the GPT-5.2 generation, following the December 2025 launch and focusing on the qualitative aspects of response delivery that shape everyday user experience.

title: Testing ads in ChatGPT (Free, Go)
content: We’re beginning to roll out a test for ads in ChatGPT in the U.S. for logged-in adults on the Free and Go tiers. Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Education plans do not have ads. The test will be a subset of users to start. We will expand over the coming weeks as we learn from this test.ChatGPT’s answers are generated independently from advertising. Answers are optimized based on what’s most helpful to you. When you see an ad, they are always clearly labeled as sponsored and visually separated from the organic answer.Ads are shown using information that stays within ChatGPT and is not shared with advertisers. This includes what you’re discussing, how you interact with ads (such as ads you hide or engage with), and your past chats and memories, if you have ads personalization enabled. You’re in control of this experience: you can dismiss ads, share feedback, learn how and why you’re being shown an ad, delete your ad data with one tap, and manage ad personalization at any time.Advertisers never see your chats or personal details and receive only aggregated performance information, like impressions and clicks. During the test, ads do not appear in chats about sensitive topics or regulated topics, including health, mental health, or politics, and are not shown to users who tell us or we predict are under 18.
title_updated: ChatGPT Ads: Begin ad test for Free & Go tier users in the U.S.
content_updated:
OpenAI begins testing advertisements in ChatGPT for logged-in adult users on the Free and Go tiers in the United States, with Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Education plans excluded from the test.
Ads are contextually shown using information that stays within ChatGPT, including current conversation topics, interaction history, and past chats with memories, and are always clearly labeled as sponsored and visually separated from organic answers. Users can dismiss ads, share feedback, delete ad data, and manage personalization at any time.
This is OpenAI's first public ad experiment in ChatGPT, potentially opening a new revenue stream beyond subscriptions, with safeguards excluding sensitive topics like health, mental health, and politics, and excluding users under 18.

title: Update on thinking time settings for GPT-5.2 Thinking in ChatGPT
content:
Jan 10, 2026:We lowered the Standard and Light thinking time as we observed users prefer faster responses. As part of this update, theExtendedthinking setting for GPT-5.2 was unintentionally changed to be lower which we have now fixed.
February 3, 2026:We made another small reduction to Standard thinking time based on testing.
February 4, 2026:We’re restoring theExtendedthinking level for GPT-5.2 Thinking to its prior setting, correcting the inadvertent reduction from January. Extended is now back to its prior level.We periodically adjust the default thinking time for our reasoning models. These changes are based on ongoing experiments to find the best balance between answer quality and response speed for users.Thethinking level toggleintroduced in September 2025 gives users more choice beyond Standard, allowing them to select the right thinking level for their question—whether they want lighter, faster responses or more extended reasoning when depth and accuracy matter more.Thinking time is not directly comparable across different models. Each model is tuned independently to what works best for users. We’ll continue to adjust these settings as models evolve, and will keep giving users clear controls when there are meaningful tradeoffs to choose from.
title_updated: GPT-5.2 Thinking: Restore Extended thinking level & update thinking time settings
content_updated:
OpenAI restores the Extended thinking level for GPT-5.2 Thinking to its prior setting, correcting an inadvertent reduction from January, while also making a small reduction to Standard thinking time based on testing.
The timeline spans from January 10 when Standard and Light thinking times were first lowered, through February 3 with another Standard reduction, to February 4 when Extended was restored to its original level.
OpenAI periodically adjusts default thinking time to balance answer quality and response speed, with the thinking level toggle introduced in September 2025 giving users explicit control over reasoning depth for their questions.

title: Introducing the Codex app
content:
Today we’re releasing the Codex app for MacOS,  a command center for managing multiple coding agents in parallel. The app lets you run long‑horizon and background tasks, review clean diffs from isolated worktrees, see agent progress and decisions, and execute reusable skills and automations.
Available with ChatGPT plans that include Codex, with a limited‑time promo: Free and Go can try Codex with included limits, while Plus, and Pro users get 2x Codex rate limits.
Get started by downloading the macOS app, andlearn moreabout Codex.
title_updated: Codex App: Launch macOS desktop client for parallel coding agents
content_updated:
OpenAI releases the Codex app for macOS, a command center for managing multiple coding agents in parallel with isolated worktrees, reviewable diffs, reusable skills, and automation capabilities.
The app is available with ChatGPT plans that include Codex, with a limited-time promotion giving Free and Go users included limits and Plus and Pro users doubled Codex rate limits.
This marks the first dedicated desktop application for Codex, moving it beyond the CLI and web interfaces into a standalone productivity tool for developers managing complex, long-horizon coding tasks.

title: More visual responses
content: Responses in ChatGPT are now more visual and easier to scan.For everyday questions, responses may include at-a-glance visuals—like checking your team’s latest stats, converting units, or doing a quick calculation.Answers now also highlight important people, places, products, and ideas. You can tap any highlight to open a side panel with key facts and trusted sources, making it easy to understand the context without asking follow-up questions.Rolling out globally on iOS, Android, web.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch visual responses with inline highlights & side panels
content_updated:
ChatGPT responses are now more visual and easier to scan, with at-a-glance visuals for everyday questions like checking stats, converting units, or quick calculations, plus highlighted key entities that users can tap for more context.
Tapping any highlighted person, place, product, or idea opens a side panel with key facts and trusted sources, enabling users to understand context without asking follow-up questions.
Rolling out globally on iOS, Android, and web, this update represents a shift toward richer, more scannable responses that reduce the need for follow-up queries.

title: Retiring GPT-4o and other legacy models
content: On February 13, 2026, alongside thepreviously announced retirement⁠ of GPT‑5 (Instant and Thinking), we will retire GPT‑4o, GPT‑4.1, GPT‑4.1 mini, and OpenAI o4-mini from ChatGPT. In the API, there are no changes at this time. For more, see ourblog postorhelp center.
title_updated: Legacy Models: Announce retirement of GPT-4o, GPT-4.1 & o4-mini effective Feb 13
content_updated:
OpenAI announces that GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and o4-mini will be retired from ChatGPT on February 13, 2026, alongside the previously announced GPT-5 Instant and Thinking retirement.
API access to these models remains unchanged; the retirement applies only to the ChatGPT consumer product, with users to be automatically migrated to GPT-5.2 as the successor.
This advance notice gives users two weeks to prepare; a follow-up execution on February 13 will confirm the completion of the retirement.

title: Improvements to search response quality in Voice
content: We’ve improved search responses in ChatGPT Voice to deliver more complete, up-to-date answers, including better access to shopping results.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Improve search response quality & shopping results
content_updated:
ChatGPT Voice now delivers more complete and up-to-date search responses, including better access to shopping results when users ask voice queries that involve product or purchase information.
The improvement targets the quality and completeness of web-sourced answers delivered through the Voice interface, making spoken responses more informative and actionable.
This update builds on ongoing Voice mode improvements, continuing to close the quality gap between text-based and voice-based search experiences in ChatGPT.

title: 5.2 Personality System Prompt Update
content: We’re updating GPT-5.2 Instant’s default personality to be more conversational and better at adapting its tone contextually, making exchanges feel smoother and more natural. You can still select a different base style and tone for ChatGPT, along with tuning characteristics like warmth and emoji use, within the Personalization menu in settings.
title_updated: GPT-5.2 Instant: Update default personality for more conversational tone
content_updated:
GPT-5.2 Instant's default personality is updated to be more conversational and better at adapting its tone contextually, making exchanges feel smoother and more natural.
Users can still select a different base style and tone through the Personalization menu in settings, along with tuning characteristics like warmth and emoji use for full control over the experience.
This personality update follows the pattern of iterative tone refinements across the GPT-5 series, building on the warmer personality introduced with GPT-5 in August 2025.

title: Voice Updates
content: We’re making an update to ChatGPT Voice for paid users that improves its ability to follow user instructions, as well as fixes a bug where Voice can sometimes repeat back custom instructions.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Improve instruction following & fix custom instruction echo bug
content_updated:
ChatGPT Voice now better follows user instructions and resolves a bug where custom instructions were sometimes repeated back verbatim during voice conversations.
This update targets the Voice mode for paid users and does not affect Advanced Voice mode or the free-tier voice experience.
This is part of ongoing Voice improvements following the November 2025 integration of Voice into the main chat interface.

title: Rolling out age prediction
content: We're rolling out an age prediction model on ChatGPT consumer plans to help determine whether an account likely belongs to someone under 18, so we can apply the right experience and safeguards for teens.The model looks at a combination of behavioral and account-level signals, including how long an account has existed, typical times of day when someone is active, usage patterns over time, and a user’s stated age.If an adult is placed in the under-18 experience by mistake, they can quickly verify their age with a selfie through Persona, a verification service available in Settings > Account. Users can check whether safeguards have been added and verify at any time.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Roll out age prediction model to apply teen safeguards
content_updated:
OpenAI rolls out an age prediction model on ChatGPT consumer plans to determine whether an account likely belongs to someone under 18, enabling appropriate safeguards and experiences for teens.
The model analyzes behavioral and account-level signals including account age, typical activity times, usage patterns over time, and the user's stated age to make its predictions.
Adults incorrectly placed in the under-18 experience can verify their age via a selfie through Persona, a verification service available in Settings, ensuring the system is correctable while prioritizing teen safety.

title: Improved memory for finding details from past chats (Plus & Pro)
content: When reference chat history is enabled, ChatGPT can now more reliably find specific details from your past chats when you ask. Any past chat used to answer your question now appears as a source so you can open and review the original context.This memory improvement is now available for Plus and Pro users globally.
title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Improve detail retrieval from past chats for Plus & Pro
content_updated:
ChatGPT can now more reliably find specific details from past chats when reference chat history is enabled, with any past chat used to answer a question appearing as a clickable source for review.
This memory improvement is available globally for Plus and Pro users, building on the enhanced memory infrastructure that references conversation history for more relevant responses.
The update makes ChatGPT's long-term memory more precise and transparent, with source attribution allowing users to verify and revisit the original context behind memory-informed answers.

title: Dictation Updates
content: We’re improving our dictation capabilities in ChatGPT for all logged-in users, significantly reducing empty transcriptions and improving accuracy.
title_updated: ChatGPT Dictation: Reduce empty transcriptions & improve accuracy
content_updated:
ChatGPT's dictation capabilities are improved for all logged-in users, significantly reducing empty transcriptions and improving overall transcription accuracy.
The update targets the voice-to-text input feature used in the message composer, making it more reliable for users who prefer speaking over typing.
This improvement addresses a common pain point where dictation would occasionally produce blank results, enhancing the reliability of voice input across all ChatGPT plans.

title: Health in ChatGPT
content:
We’re starting to roll outHealth, a dedicated space in ChatGPT for health and wellness conversations. You can securely connect medical records, Apple Health and supported wellness apps so answers can be grounded in your own data. Health is designed to help you navigate medical care, not replace it. Health conversations, memory, and files are kept separate from the rest of ChatGPT and are not used to train our foundation models.
Health appears as a new space in the ChatGPT sidebar. If you ask a health-related question in a regular chat, ChatGPT may also suggest continuing in Health. If you’re interested in getting access as it becomes available, you can sign up for thewaitlist. We’re starting by providing access to a small group of early users to learn and continue refining the experience. At launch, Health is available onweb and iOS, with Android coming soon toChatGPT Free, Go, Plus, and Pro usersin supported countries (excluding the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK).
Learn more in theblogorhelp center article.
title_updated: ChatGPT Health: Launch dedicated health & wellness space with medical records
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces Health, a dedicated space for health and wellness conversations that securely connects to medical records, Apple Health, and supported wellness apps so answers can be grounded in the user's own data.
Health appears as a new sidebar space with conversations, memory, and files kept separate from the rest of ChatGPT and not used for model training. At launch, it is available on web and iOS with Android coming soon for Free, Go, Plus, and Pro users in supported countries.
This marks ChatGPT's first dedicated vertical feature for healthcare, rolling out gradually to a small group of early users with a waitlist, as part of OpenAI's push into domain-specific experiences.

title: Your Year with ChatGPT
content:
Today we’re rolling outYour Year with ChatGPT, an optional, personalized end-of-year experience that reflects on how you interacted with ChatGPT in 2025. It highlights high-level themes from your conversations and includes summary statistics about your usage over the year.
This experience is rolling out gradually throughout the day, so it may not be available to everyone immediately. It’s available to Free, Plus, and Pro users in United States, United Kingdom, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand as well as Go, Plus, and Pro users in India, and is not available on Business, Enterprise, or Edu plans.
To seeYour Year with ChatGPT, Memory and Reference Chat History must be turned on, and you must meet a minimum activity threshold. If you have very limited activity, you’ll only see basic chat statistics.
At launch, this experience is available in English in the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch Your Year with ChatGPT 2025 retrospective experience
content_updated:
OpenAI launches Your Year with ChatGPT, an optional personalized end-of-year experience reflecting on how users interacted with ChatGPT in 2025, including high-level conversation themes and usage statistics.
Available to Free, Plus, and Pro users in the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand, plus Go, Plus, and Pro users in India, it requires Memory and Reference Chat History to be enabled with a minimum activity threshold.
This marks ChatGPT's first annual retrospective feature, similar to year-in-review experiences from other platforms, rolling out gradually in English and excluded from Business, Enterprise, and Edu plans.

title: New detailed characteristic controls
content:
In addition to selecting a base style and tone preset—like candid and friendly—you can now adjust specific characteristics individually.  From the ‘personalization’ pane in settings, you can adjust ChatGPT to:
Be more or less warm
Be more or less enthusiastic
Use more or fewer headers & lists
Use more or fewer emojis
title_updated: ChatGPT Personalization: Add granular characteristic controls for warmth & style
content_updated:
ChatGPT now offers detailed characteristic controls beyond base style and tone presets, allowing users to individually adjust warmth, enthusiasm, use of headers and lists, and emoji frequency.
These controls are accessible from the Personalization pane in settings and work alongside existing presets like candid and friendly for more fine-grained control over response style.
This extends the personalization system launched earlier in 2025, giving users more precise control over how ChatGPT communicates rather than relying solely on preset tone profiles.

title: Introducing the app directory in ChatGPT
content: You can now browse and add approved apps in the newapp directory. Apps let you work with your tools and data directly inside a conversation—from interactive in-chat experiences to secure connections that allow ChatGPT to search and reference information from third-party services.As part of this update,connectors now appear in the directory as apps, making it easier to manage all your tools in one place. References across the Help Center have been updated to reflect this new terminology.Apps are available to all logged-in ChatGPT users, with availability and functionality varying by plan and region. Some apps or capabilities may not be available in certain regions or may require specific plan tiers.Additionally,developers can submit apps for review and publicationin the app directory, making approved apps available to a broader set of ChatGPT users.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch app directory for browsing & adding third-party apps
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces an app directory where users can browse and add approved apps that work with tools and data directly inside conversations, from interactive in-chat experiences to secure third-party data connections.
Existing connectors now appear in the directory as apps, consolidating tool management in one place, and developers can submit apps for review and publication to reach the broader ChatGPT user base.
Apps are available to all logged-in users with availability varying by plan and region, marking a significant step toward ChatGPT as a platform for third-party integrations beyond the earlier GPT Store concept.

title: Pinned chats in ChatGPT
content: You can now pin chats to the top of your list for quick access to important conversations. Pinned chats are available on iOS, Android, Web, and Atlas.On web, hover over a chat in the left-hand sidebar, click ⋯, and select Pin chat. On mobile, long-press a chat and select Pin chat.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch pinned chats for quick access across all platforms
content_updated:
ChatGPT now lets users pin chats to the top of their conversation list for quick access to important conversations, available on iOS, Android, Web, and Atlas.
On web, users hover over a chat in the sidebar and select Pin chat from the menu; on mobile, a long-press on a chat reveals the pin option.
This addresses a frequently requested feature for users managing many conversations, making it easier to keep key discussions accessible without scrolling through history.

title: Tasks are now in Pulse
content:
Tasks let you set up automated prompts that ChatGPT can run for you.You can now manage all your tasks directly in Pulse:
See all tasks in one place in Pulse.
Create tasks in chat or in Pulse—whatever’s easier in the moment.
Edit and review tasks in Pulse anytime after you create them.Please note that Pulse is only available in ChatGPT Pro.
title_updated: ChatGPT Pulse: Move task management into Pulse for Pro users
content_updated:
ChatGPT Tasks, which let users set up automated prompts that run at scheduled times, can now be managed directly within Pulse alongside creation, editing, and review capabilities.
Users can create tasks in chat or in Pulse and edit them anytime after creation, with all tasks visible in one centralized location within the Pulse interface.
This consolidation is exclusive to ChatGPT Pro users, integrating the previously separate Tasks feature into the Pulse dashboard launched in September 2025 for a more unified workflow experience.

title: ChatGPT Images on Web and Mobile (iOS & Android)
content:
We’ve introduced a new and improved version ofChatGPT Images, powered by our best image generation model yet. With ChatGPT Images, precise instruction following and editing makes AI image creation not just more fun, but meaningfully more useful—from everyday edits to creative transformations.
All images you create with ChatGPT are automatically saved underMy imagesathttps://chatgpt.com/images, giving you a single place to browse, revisit, and reuse your work without having to sift through past conversations.
Learn more about ChatGPT Images in ourChatGPT Images FAQ.
Available onFree, Go, Plus, Edu, and Proplans. Business and Enterprise are coming soon!
title_updated: ChatGPT Images: Launch improved image generation on Web & Mobile (iOS & Android)
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces a new and improved image generation experience powered by its best model yet, with precise instruction following and editing capabilities that make AI image creation more useful for everyday edits and creative transformations.
All generated images are automatically saved to My Images at chatgpt.com/images, providing a single place to browse, revisit, and reuse work without searching through past conversations.
Available on Free, Go, Plus, Edu, and Pro plans with Business and Enterprise coming soon, this update represents a major upgrade to ChatGPT's visual creation capabilities.

title: Voice on macOS desktop app is retiring (effective January 15, 2026)
content:
We’re retiring the Voice experience in the ChatGPT macOS app on January 15, 2026. This change allows us to focus on more unified and improved voice experiences across our apps.
Voice will continue to be available onchatgpt.com, iOS, Android, and Windows app.
No other ChatGPT features on macOS are affected.
title_updated: ChatGPT macOS: Announce Voice retirement effective January 15, 2026
content_updated:
OpenAI announces the retirement of the Voice experience in the ChatGPT macOS desktop app, effective January 15, 2026, to focus on more unified voice experiences across other platforms.
Voice will continue to be available on chatgpt.com, iOS, Android, and the Windows app, with no other ChatGPT features on macOS affected by this change.
This retirement consolidates voice functionality onto platforms where it can be maintained and improved more effectively, following the November 2025 unification of Voice into the main chat interface.

title: Today, we’re releasingGPT-5.2, the next upgrade to the GPT-5 series. GPT-5.2 is smarter and more use
content: Today, we’re releasingGPT-5.2, the next upgrade to the GPT-5 series. GPT-5.2 is smarter and more useful for both work and learning—while remaining just as enjoyable to talk to. As with GPT-5.1, we’re continuing to improve our main models for everyone on a regular basis to make them more useful and enjoyable to use.GPT-5.2 Instantis a fast yet powerful workhorse for everyday work and learning, with clear improvements in info-seeking questions, how-tos and walk-throughs, technical writing, and translation, all while retaining the warmer, more conversational tone introduced in GPT-5.1 Instant. Early testers noted its more clearly structured explanations where important info is highlighted upfront. It’s also more effective at supporting studying and skill-building, as well as offering clearer job and career guidance.GPT-5.2 Thinkingsolves harder work tasks more effectively and with more polish—particularly in spreadsheet formatting and financial modeling, alongside improvements in slideshow creation. Early testing showed gains in coding, summarizing long documents, answering questions about uploaded files, walking through complex math and logic step by step, and helping with planning and decision-making with clearer structure and thoughtful detail.GPT-5.2 Prois our smartest and most trustworthy model yet for difficult questions where a higher-quality answer is worth the wait. In early testing, it shows fewer major errors and stronger performance across complex domains like programming.All three models (Instant, Thinking, and Pro) have a new knowledge cutoff of August 2025. For users, this means GPT-5.2 starts with a more current understanding of the world, so answers are more accurate and useful, with more relevant examples and context, even before turning to web search.Read ourblogfor more details on examples and evals.Update for free and Go users:We’re removing automatic model switching for reasoning in ChatGPT. Previously, some questions were automatically routed to the Thinking model when ChatGPT determined it might help. To maximize choice, free users will now useGPT-5.2 Instantby default, and can still choose to use reasoning anytime by selectingThinkingfrom the tools menu in the message composer.
title_updated: GPT-5.2: Launch smarter GPT-5 series upgrade with Instant, Thinking & Pro variants
content_updated:
OpenAI releases GPT-5.2 as the next upgrade in the GPT-5 series, offering improved intelligence for work and learning across three variants: Instant for everyday tasks, Thinking for harder problems, and Pro for the most difficult questions.
GPT-5.2 Instant improves info-seeking, how-tos, technical writing, and translation while keeping a conversational tone; Thinking excels at spreadsheet formatting, financial modeling, and coding; Pro delivers fewer major errors across complex domains. All three models have a new knowledge cutoff of August 2025.
For free and Go users, automatic model switching for reasoning is removed, with GPT-5.2 Instant as the default and Thinking available as an explicit choice, giving users more control over model selection.

title: An improved Voice interface: the audio you know, with text and visuals when you need them.
content:
We’re making ChatGPT Voice a seamless part of the ChatGPT experience, so you can use voice right inside the chat interface you already use every day—no separate mode required.
Many of you have asked for a more flexible Voice experience—now, you can speak, listen, and see your conversation at the same time. Answers will be spoken alongside streamed text, image search results, widgets (maps, weather, etc.) and more—right in the same chat thread.
This makes voice chats clearer, richer and more contextual by pairing spoken answers with visual context. You can:
Follow along with typed answers as you listen
See visual search results like images, charts, and map cards in real time
Refer back to earlier messages without interrupting the conversation
Type back when you can’t talk and still get a Voice response
Share photos to discuss them out loud
This means Voice can better adapt to your day and meet you where you are, whether you're practicing a language and seeing words spelled out, need to refer to a measurement while cooking hands-free, or talking through a chart or code sample where visuals help.
This update is rolling out to all users globally on the ChatGPT mobile app and chatgpt.com. If you prefer the original Voice experience, you can switch back anytime by going to Settings and turning on “Separate mode” under Voice Mode. We’ll continue listening and refining this experience as we build toward Voice interactions that are both integrated and immersive.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Integrate voice into main chat interface with text & visual responses
content_updated:
ChatGPT Voice is redesigned as a seamless part of the main chat interface, allowing users to speak, listen, and see conversations simultaneously without requiring a separate mode.
Answers are spoken alongside streamed text, image search results, and widgets like maps and weather in the same chat thread, enabling users to follow typed answers while listening, see visual results in real time, type back when unable to talk, and share photos for voice discussion.
Rolling out globally on mobile and chatgpt.com, users who prefer the original experience can switch back via Settings by enabling Separate mode, while this integration marks a major step toward unified multimodal interactions.

title: Shopping research in ChatGPT
content:
Shopping research is a new interactive experience that helps you find the right products without doing all the research yourself. Describe what you need—like a quiet cordless vacuum for a small apartment, a gift for a four‑year‑old who loves art, or a way to choose between three bikes—and ChatGPT will ask clarifying questions, research deeply across the internet, review quality sources, and build on ChatGPT’s understanding of you from past conversations and memory to return a personalized buyer’s guide with options, tradeoffs, and links to buy.
ChatGPT learns what you like through an interactive interface allowing you to guide the research based on your real-time feedback.
Shopping research is available on ChatGPT Web, iOS, and Android for logged‑in Free, Go, Plus, and Pro users. To help with holiday shopping, we’re making usage nearly unlimited across plans through the season. To learn more, visit theblog.
title_updated: ChatGPT Shopping: Launch interactive research for personalized buying guides
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces Shopping Research, an interactive experience where users describe their needs and ChatGPT asks clarifying questions, researches the internet, reviews quality sources, and builds a personalized buyer's guide with options, tradeoffs, and purchase links.
The experience leverages memory from past conversations and allows real-time feedback to guide research, available on Web, iOS, and Android for logged-in Free, Go, Plus, and Pro users with nearly unlimited usage through the holiday season.
This represents ChatGPT's first dedicated shopping workflow, going beyond simple product search to provide an end-to-end research assistant that personalizes recommendations based on user preferences and constraints.

title: More images in answers
content:
ChatGPT now adds more inline images from the web to help you quickly understand topics like well-known people, places, and products. When visuals can add clarity, you’ll see images placed beside the relevant paragraph. You can click on any of the images to see them in original dimensions and with the source attribution.
These improvements are gradually rolling out globally across all ChatGPT plans, on web, iOS, and Android for responses using GPT 5.1.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Add more inline web images for people, places & products
content_updated:
ChatGPT now includes more inline images from the web alongside relevant paragraphs to help users quickly understand topics involving well-known people, places, and products, with clickable images showing original dimensions and source attribution.
The improvements are gradually rolling out globally across all ChatGPT plans on web, iOS, and Android for responses using GPT-5.1, adding visual clarity where images can enhance understanding.
This builds on the broader push toward more visual and scannable responses, making ChatGPT answers feel more like rich reference pages than plain text outputs.

title: Instant Checkout now rolling out to Shopify merchants
content:
You can now find and buy from Shopify merchants starting with Spanx, Skims, and Glossier through Instant Checkout in ChatGPT.
When a product from a Shopify merchant supports Instant Checkout, you can complete the purchase right in thechat with a quick confirmation of your shipping and payment details.
Instant Checkout is available for logged in Plus, Pro, and Free users in the US.
title_updated: Instant Checkout: Expand to Shopify merchants starting with Spanx, Skims & Glossier
content_updated:
ChatGPT Instant Checkout now supports purchases from Shopify merchants, starting with Spanx, Skims, and Glossier, allowing users to complete purchases directly in chat with a quick confirmation of shipping and payment details.
Instant Checkout is available for logged-in Plus, Pro, and Free users in the US, with the purchase flow triggered when a product from a supported Shopify merchant is available.
This expansion follows the initial Etsy launch in September 2025, bringing over one million Shopify merchants into the Instant Checkout ecosystem and significantly broadening the range of products available for in-chat purchasing.

title: GPT-5.1 Pro: Clearer, more capable answers for complex work
content:
Today we’re updating GPT-5 Pro to GPT-5.1 Pro. In early testing, users consistently preferred GPT-5.1 Pro over GPT-5 Pro, rating it especially highly for writing help, data science, and business questions. Testers highlighted the model’s improved clarity, relevance, and structure in its responses.
GPT-5.1 Pro is rolling out today for all ChatGPT Pro users and is available in the model picker. GPT-5 Pro will remain available as a legacy model for 90 days before being retired.
title_updated: GPT-5.1 Pro: Launch upgraded model with improved clarity & relevance
content_updated:
OpenAI updates GPT-5 Pro to GPT-5.1 Pro, with early testers consistently preferring it over GPT-5 Pro and rating it especially highly for writing help, data science, and business questions due to improved clarity, relevance, and structure.
GPT-5.1 Pro is rolling out to all ChatGPT Pro users and available in the model picker, while GPT-5 Pro remains accessible as a legacy model for 90 days before retirement.
This follows the broader GPT-5.1 upgrade pattern, bringing the Pro tier's most capable model in line with the Instant and Thinking variants that were updated earlier in November 2025.

title: Group chats in ChatGPT
content:
We’re piloting group chats so you can bring people - and ChatGPT - into the same conversation. Plan trips, make decisions, and collaborate on projects together while ChatGPT helps search, summarize, generate content, and keep everyone on the same page.
Group chats are starting to roll out on ChatGPT Web, iOS, and Android in New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan. We’ll use early feedback from this pilot to improve the experience and inform how we expand to more regions and plans over time. To learn more visit theblog.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch group chats pilot in New Zealand, Japan, South Korea & Taiwan
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces a pilot of group chats, allowing multiple people and ChatGPT to collaborate in the same conversation for trip planning, decision-making, and project work while ChatGPT helps search, summarize, and generate content.
The pilot is rolling out on ChatGPT Web, iOS, and Android in New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan, with early feedback informing how the feature expands to more regions and plans over time.
This marks ChatGPT's first real-time collaborative feature, moving beyond single-user interactions toward a shared workspace model where multiple participants can leverage AI assistance simultaneously.

title: GPT-5.1 in ChatGPT
content:
We’re updating GPT-5 to GPT-5.1 Instant and GPT-5.1 Thinking, making answers both smarter and more conversational. GPT-5.1 Instant now uses light adaptive reasoning for tougher questions while staying fast, and GPT-5.1 Thinking adapts its thinking time more precisely for complex tasks with clearer, less jargony responses.
GPT-5.1 Auto continues to route each query to the best model, and GPT-5 models remain available for three months under the Legacy models dropdown so you can compare and transition at your own pace.
GPT-5.1 Instant and Thinking are rolling out first to paid (Pro, Plus, Go, Business) users, then to free and logged-out users, with Enterprise and Edu getting a temporary early-access toggle; GPT-5.1 will also be available soon in the API as gpt-5.1-chat-latest (Instant) and gpt-5.1 (Thinking).
title_updated: GPT-5.1: Launch smarter Instant & Thinking models with adaptive reasoning
content_updated:
OpenAI updates GPT-5 to GPT-5.1 Instant and GPT-5.1 Thinking, with Instant now using light adaptive reasoning for tougher questions while staying fast, and Thinking adapting its reasoning time more precisely for complex tasks with clearer, less jargony responses.
GPT-5.1 Auto continues routing queries to the best model, and GPT-5 models remain available for three months under Legacy models. Rolling out first to paid users, then to free and logged-out users, with Enterprise and Edu getting temporary early-access toggles.
GPT-5.1 will also be available in the API as gpt-5.1-chat-latest for Instant and gpt-5.1 for Thinking, continuing the rapid iterative improvement cadence within the GPT-5 generation.

title: New tone and personalization controls
content:
We’re updating ChatGPT’s tone controls so it’s easier to make responses sound the way you prefer. You can now choose from updated presets like Default, Friendly, Efficient, Professional, Candid, and Quirky (with Cynical and Nerdy still available), and we’re experimenting with more granular controls over how concise, warm, or scannable responses are, plus how often emojis appear.
Changes you make in Personalization now apply across all chats immediately (including existing conversations), and GPT-5.1 models follow your custom instructions and style preferences more reliably.
title_updated: ChatGPT Personalization: Launch updated tone presets & granular style controls
content_updated:
ChatGPT updates its tone controls with new presets including Default, Friendly, Efficient, Professional, Candid, and Quirky, while introducing experimental granular controls over conciseness, warmth, scannability, and emoji frequency.
Changes made in Personalization now apply immediately across all chats including existing conversations, and GPT-5.1 models follow custom instructions and style preferences more reliably than before.
This represents a significant expansion of user control over ChatGPT's communication style, building on the personality system introduced with GPT-5 and making personalization changes instant rather than requiring new conversations.

title: Personality and custom instructions now apply to all chats
content: Previously, changes you made to your ChatGPT personality or custom instructions only applied to new conversations. Starting today, updates you make in Settings → Personalization are applied immediately across all chats (including existing conversations!) so your experience stays consistent everywhere without needing to start a new thread.
title_updated: ChatGPT Personalization: Apply personality & custom instructions to all existing chats
content_updated:
ChatGPT now applies changes to personality and custom instructions immediately across all chats, including existing conversations, rather than only new conversations as was previously the case.
This update ensures a consistent experience everywhere without requiring users to start new threads when they change their personalization settings.
Previously, users had to start new conversations to see personalization changes take effect, creating an inconsistent experience across old and new chats that this update resolves.

title: Interrupt ChatGPT
content: You can now interrupt long-running queries (such as deep research or GPT-5 Pro) to refine what you’re asking—no restart required or progress lost. For example, if you're researching the perfect bookshelf and suddenly remember that your wall is 72" wide and you can't drill into it, you can simply add that information midstream by clicking “update” in the sidebar and sending the new information as a message. The model will adjust its approach based on your new requirements.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch mid-query interruption for deep research & long-running tasks
content_updated:
ChatGPT now allows users to interrupt long-running queries like deep research or GPT-5 Pro to refine their question mid-stream without restarting or losing progress.
Users click an update button in the sidebar to send new information as a message, and the model adjusts its approach based on the new requirements, enabling real-time course correction during extended reasoning sessions.
This feature addresses a common frustration with long-running queries where users realize mid-way that they need to add constraints or change direction, previously requiring a complete restart.

title: ChatGPT Go Promo - India
content:
Eligible customers in India can get now get 12 months ofChatGPT Goat no cost. new New users canredeem nowon ChatGPT web or the Android app (Google Play). The Apple App Store option arrives next week - no promo code is required for redemption.
Available to users in India who are new, on the free plan, or are current ChatGPT Go subscribers - one redemption per account. Requires a payment method (credit card or UPI).
If you have an existing ChatGPT Go membership, we may be able to automatically add the promotion for you at the end of your current billing cycle - reviewChatGPT Go Promotion (India)for more details.Update: as of January 21, 2026, the promo has ended
title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Launch 12-month free promotion for eligible users in India
content_updated:
Eligible customers in India can redeem 12 months of ChatGPT Go at no cost on ChatGPT web or Android app via Google Play, with the Apple App Store option arriving the following week and no promo code required.
The promotion is available to new users, free plan users, and current ChatGPT Go subscribers in India, limited to one redemption per account and requiring a payment method such as credit card or UPI.
This promotion targets the Indian market where ChatGPT Go originally launched in August 2025, aiming to accelerate adoption in one of OpenAI's key growth regions. The promo ended as of January 21, 2026.

title: ChatGPT Go expansion
content:
We're excited to announce that ChatGPT Go, our low cost subscription plan, is now available in 8 additional countries in Europe, bringing total coverage to98 countries. The new countries we added are Austria, Czech Republic, Denmark, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Spain and Sweden.
For an affordable price, ChatGPT Go provides everything included in theFreeplan, and:
More messages, larger file uploads, and expanded image generation
Access to advanced data analysis
Longer memory for more personalized responses
Available on web, mobile (iOS & Android), and desktop (macOS & Windows).
Learn more aboutChatGPT Go.
title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Expand to 8 additional European countries, reaching 98 total
content_updated:
ChatGPT Go expands to eight additional European countries including Austria, Czech Republic, Denmark, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Spain, and Sweden, bringing total availability to 98 countries.
The low-cost subscription plan provides more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory beyond the Free plan, available across web, mobile, and desktop.
This European expansion follows the October rollout to 71 additional countries and continues OpenAI's strategy of making an affordable paid tier available globally.

title: Credits for Flexible Usage for Codex and Sora
content: Today we’re introducing ways to purchase additional usage in products like Codex and Sora. If you hit your included limits, you can seamlessly buy more credits right in the Codex dashboard or buy more video generations in the Sora app to keep going. Learn more in our article:Using Credits for Flexible Usage in ChatGPT (Free/Go/Plus/Pro) & Sora.
title_updated: Codex & Sora: Launch credits for flexible usage beyond included limits
content_updated:
OpenAI introduces purchasable credits for additional usage in Codex and Sora, allowing users who hit their included limits to seamlessly buy more directly within the Codex dashboard or Sora app.
Credits are available for ChatGPT Free, Go, Plus, and Pro plans, providing a pay-as-you-go option that extends beyond the fixed rate limits included with each subscription tier.
This marks OpenAI's first usage-based billing model alongside subscriptions, creating a hybrid monetization approach for compute-intensive features like coding agents and video generation.

title: ChatGPT Pulse on Web
content:
ChatGPT Pulse is now available on web and rolling out to Atlas for Pro users.
With Pulse, ChatGPT can do asynchronous research on your behalf once a day based on your past chats, memory, and feedback to help you get things done. The research is delivered to you proactively the next day as a series of visual summaries that you can scan at a glance, expand for more detail, save for later, or ask follow up questions.
title_updated: ChatGPT Pulse: Launch on Web & Atlas with daily asynchronous research summaries
content_updated:
ChatGPT Pulse is now available on web and rolling out to Atlas for Pro users, enabling asynchronous daily research based on past chats, memory, and feedback, delivered as visual summaries the next day.
Users can scan summaries at a glance, expand for more detail, save for later, or ask follow-up questions, with Pulse synthesizing information proactively based on the user's context and interests.
This web launch follows the initial iOS and Android release in September 2025, expanding Pulse's proactive intelligence feature to desktop users for a more complete cross-platform experience.

title: ChatGPT Go now available in Brazil
content:
We're launching ChatGPT Go, our low cost subscription plan, in Brazil. ChatGPT Go provides everything included in theFreeplan, and:
More messages, larger file uploads, and expanded image generation
Access to advanced data analysis
Longer memory for more personalized responses
Available on web, mobile (iOS & Android), and desktop (macOS & Windows).
Learn more aboutChatGPT Go.
title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Launch low-cost subscription plan in Brazil
content_updated:
ChatGPT Go launches in Brazil, offering an affordable subscription that includes more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory beyond the Free plan.
The plan is available across web, mobile on iOS and Android, and desktop on macOS and Windows, matching the feature set of Go plans in other markets.
Brazil becomes the latest market for ChatGPT Go as OpenAI continues its global expansion of the low-cost tier, following launches in India, Indonesia, and dozens of other countries throughout 2025.

title: Shared projects
content:
Starting today, project sharing is available to all ChatGPT users, including for Free, Plus, Pro, and Go users globally on web, iOS, and Android.
ChatGPT can draw from anything in the shared project – including chats, uploaded files and custom instructions – so responses are informed by your group's internal knowledge, and you can pick up where others left off.
Shared projects are ideal for:
Group work: Upload notes, proposals, and contracts so collaborators can draft deliverables faster and stay in sync.
Content creation: Apply project-specific instructions to keep tone and style consistent across contributors.
Reporting: Store datasets and reports in one project, and return each week to generate updates without starting over.
Research: Keep transcripts, survey results, and market research in one place, so anyone in the project can query and build on the findings.
Project owners can choose to share a project with “Only those invited” or “Anyone with a link,” and can change visibility settings at any time including switching back to invite-only. The following collaboration limits apply:
Pro users:up to 40 files and 100 collaborators
Plus and Go users:up to 25 files and 10 collaborators
Free users:up to 5 files and 5 collaborators
title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Launch shared projects with collaboration for all plans
content_updated:
ChatGPT shared projects are now available to all users including Free, Plus, Pro, and Go plans globally on web, iOS, and Android, enabling group collaboration where ChatGPT draws from shared chats, files, and custom instructions.
Collaboration limits vary by plan: Pro supports up to 40 files and 100 collaborators, Plus and Go support 25 files and 10 collaborators, and Free supports 5 files and 5 collaborators, with project owners controlling visibility settings.
Shared projects enable use cases like group work, content creation, reporting, and research, representing ChatGPT's first team collaboration feature available across all subscription tiers.

title: We’re updating the model for signed-out users to GPT-5 Instant, giving more people access to higher-
content: We’re updating the model for signed-out users to GPT-5 Instant, giving more people access to higher-quality responses by default.See hereto learn more about the intelligence of GPT-5.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Upgrade signed-out users to GPT-5 Instant model
content_updated:
ChatGPT updates the default model for signed-out users to GPT-5 Instant, giving more people access to higher-quality responses without requiring account creation or login.
This change applies the same GPT-5 Instant model used by logged-in free users to the signed-out experience, removing the quality gap between authenticated and unauthenticated usage.
The upgrade continues OpenAI's strategy of democratizing access to its best models, following the pattern of making each new model generation the default for progressively wider audiences.

title: ChatGPT can now automatically manage saved memories so it’s even better at remembering what’s important to you.
content:
ChatGPT now manages saved memories automatically by keeping the most relevant details prioritized and moving less important ones to the background. This helps prevent saved memories from reaching capacity and helps avoid “memory full” state in your ChatGPT account.
To decide which memories stay top of mind, ChatGPT considers factors such as how recent a detail is and how often you talk about a topic. You can now also search your saved memories more easily and sort them by newest or oldest.
You’re always in control: you can turn off automatic memory management at any time, and you can see which memories are currently top of mind and choose to prioritize or deprioritize any specific memory yourself in settings. You can also view and restore prior versions of saved memories.
These updates are designed to make saved memory feel seamless, reliable, and easy to manage. We’re beginning to roll this out to Plus and Pro users globally on the web.
title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Launch automatic memory management for Plus & Pro users
content_updated:
ChatGPT now automatically manages saved memories by keeping the most relevant details prioritized and moving less important ones to the background, preventing the memory full state that previously required manual cleanup.
The system considers factors like recency and topic frequency to decide which memories stay top of mind, with users able to search, sort, prioritize, deprioritize, and restore prior versions of saved memories.
Rolling out to Plus and Pro users globally on web, this update makes memory management seamless while keeping users in control, with the ability to turn off automatic management at any time.

title: ChatGPT Go expansion
content:
We're excited to announce that ChatGPT Go, our low cost subscription plan, is now available in71 additional countries, bringing total coverage to89 countries.
For an affordable price, ChatGPT Go provides everything included in theFreeplan, and:
More messages, larger file uploads, and expanded image generation
Access to advanced data analysis
Longer memory for more personalized responses
Available on web, mobile (iOS & Android), and desktop (macOS & Windows).
Note:In Cambodia, Laos, and Nepal, ChatGPT Go subscriptions aren’t available in the iOS app currently. Web and Android subscriptions remain available.
Learn more aboutChatGPT Go.
title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Expand to 71 additional countries, reaching 89 total
content_updated:
ChatGPT Go expands to 71 additional countries, bringing total availability to 89 countries, with the affordable subscription offering more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory.
The plan is available across web, mobile on iOS and Android, and desktop on macOS and Windows, though iOS app subscriptions are not available in Cambodia, Laos, and Nepal where web and Android remain available.
This massive expansion significantly broadens ChatGPT Go's global reach following its initial launch in India in August 2025 and subsequent single-country rollouts.

title: Slack connector and app
content:
We’re rolling out two new ways to bring Slack and ChatGPT together: aconnectorthat brings your Slack context into ChatGPT, and a ChatGPT app for Slack – an integration that enables you to chat with ChatGPT from inside Slack.
With theChatGPT app for Slack,you can chat one-on-one with ChatGPT in a dedicated Slack sidebar – a space to ask questions, summarize long threads into action items, draft replies, and search messages and files you already have access to. Chats you start in Slack also appear in your ChatGPT sidebar, so it’s easy to pick up later from web or mobile. Semantic search is supported for Slack customers with AI enabled on Business+ or Enterprise+ plans; all other plans use keyword search.
With theChatGPT connector for Slack,you can securely bring in context from your Slack channels and DMs, making responses more helpful Available in chat, Deep Research, and Agent Mode. Users can enable fromSettings > Apps & Connectors.
Both app and connector are available toPlus, Pro, Business and Enterprise/Educustomers. Additionally, the ChatGPT app for Slack requires apaid Slack account;availability and workspace installation may depend on your Slack workspace settings.
Installing the app requires the connector to be enabled for your account.
Visit theChatGPT app for Slackpage to get started with installation.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch Slack connector & Slack app integration for Plus, Pro & Business
content_updated:
OpenAI launches two Slack integrations: a connector that brings Slack context into ChatGPT conversations, and a ChatGPT app for Slack that enables direct interaction with ChatGPT from within the Slack sidebar.
The Slack app supports one-on-one chats, thread summarization, action item extraction, reply drafting, and message search, with chats synced to the ChatGPT sidebar for cross-platform continuity. The connector enables secure context from channels and DMs in chat and Deep Research.
Both integrations are available to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise/Edu customers, with the Slack app requiring a paid Slack account and the connector required before installing the app.

title: Adding Notion & Linear synced connectors
content: We're addingNotionandLinearas synced connectors (for Pro users), so you can securely bring Notion pages and Linear issues/discussions into Chat for fast answers and summaries. Once synced, ChatGPT will automatically reference the indexed content when relevant. Learn more aboutsynced connectors.
title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Add Notion & Linear as synced connectors for Pro users
content_updated:
Pro users can now connect Notion and Linear as synced connectors, enabling ChatGPT to automatically reference indexed Notion pages and Linear issues and discussions when relevant in conversations.
Users enable sync from Connector settings after connecting to Notion or Linear, and ChatGPT will automatically reference the indexed content without manual selection for each query.
This adds two popular productivity tools to the connector ecosystem, expanding Pro users' ability to ground ChatGPT responses in their actual project data and documentation.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Expand to 16 additional countries with affordable subscription plan
