title: Updates to Codex
content:
We’re rolling out new Codex capabilities to help teams work and build better:Codex now works in Slack, and supports programmatic control through theCodex SDK. Additionally, we've updatedCodex rates.
For more information, review our Help Center article onCodex, and theCodex developer documentation.
title_updated: Codex: Launch Slack integration, SDK & updated rate limits
content_updated:
Codex rolls out new capabilities including Slack integration for team workflows, programmatic control through the Codex SDK, and updated rate limits for managing usage.
The Slack integration allows developers to interact with Codex directly from their team communication tool, while the SDK enables automated and programmatic task management beyond the web and CLI interfaces.
These updates position Codex as a more team-oriented and automatable development tool, expanding its reach beyond individual developer workflows into broader team collaboration and CI/CD-style automation.

title: Updating GPT-5
content:
We’re updatingGPT-5 Instantto better recognize and support people in moments of distress.
The model is trained to more accurately detect and respond to potential signs of mental and emotional distress. These updates were guided by mental health experts, and help ChatGPT de-escalate conversations and point people to real-world crisis resources when appropriate, while still using language that feels supportive and grounding.
As we shared in arecent blog, we've been using our real-time router to direct sensitive parts of conversations—such as those showing signs of acute distress—to reasoning models. GPT-5 Instant now performs just as well as GPT-5 Thinking on these types of questions. When GPT-5 Auto or a non-reasoning model is selected, we'll instead route these conversations to GPT-5 Instant to more quickly provide helpful and beneficial responses. ChatGPT will continue to tell users which model is active when asked.
This update to GPT-5 Instant is starting to roll out to ChatGPT users today.We’re continuing to work on improvements and will keep updating the model to make it smarter and safer over time.
title_updated: GPT-5 Instant: Improve mental distress detection & crisis response
content_updated:
GPT-5 Instant is updated to better recognize and support people in moments of distress, with improved detection and response to potential signs of mental and emotional distress guided by mental health experts.
GPT-5 Instant now performs as well as GPT-5 Thinking on sensitive conversations, and the real-time router directs distress-related parts of conversations to GPT-5 Instant even when a non-reasoning model is selected.
This safety update reflects ongoing work to make ChatGPT's responses supportive and grounding during crisis moments, building on research shared in OpenAI's blog about reducing sycophancy while maintaining warmth.

title: Parental Controls
content:
We’re rolling out parental controls globally in ChatGPT to help families guide how ChatGPT works in their homes. Available starting on the web today, mobile soon.
To set up parental controls, a parent or guardian sends an invite to their teen to connect accounts. After the teen accepts, the parent can manage the teen’s settings from their own account. Teens can also invite a parent to connect.
Once a teen is connected to a parent’s account, they automatically receive additional content protections, such as reduced graphic content and viral challenges. Parents will have the option to turn this setting off if they choose, but teen users cannot make changes.
Parents don’t have access to their teen’s conversations, except in rare cases where our system and trained reviewers detect possible signs of serious safety risk, parents may be notified — but only with the information needed to support their teen’s safety. Parents can choose if they’d like to be contacted by email, SMS, push notification, or all three.
Parents can adjust certain features, set time limits, and add safeguards that work for their family including:
Set quiet hours, or specific times when ChatGPT can’t be used.
Turn off voice mode, to remove the option to use voice mode in ChatGPT.
Turn off memory, so ChatGPT won’t save and use memories when responding.
Remove image generation, so ChatGPT won’t have the ability to create or edit images.
Opt out of model training,so their teen’s conversations won’t be used to improve models powering ChatGPT and Sora.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch parental controls for teen account management globally
content_updated:
OpenAI rolls out parental controls globally in ChatGPT, allowing parents or guardians to connect accounts with teens and manage settings including content protections, feature access, time limits, and usage safeguards.
Parents can set quiet hours, disable voice mode, turn off memory, remove image generation, and opt out of model training, while connected teens automatically receive additional content protections that only parents can disable.
In rare cases where safety systems detect possible signs of serious risk, parents may be notified with limited information needed to support their teen's safety, with notification preferences configurable via email, SMS, or push notification.

title: Introducing Instant Checkout and the Agentic Commerce Protocol
content:
We’re introducing Instant Checkout in ChatGPT and the Agentic Commerce Protocol that powers it, built with Stripe.
ChatGPT can help you find anything on the web and now with Instant Checkout, you can seamlessly buy directly from the merchant, right in chat. Starting today, U.S. ChatGPT Plus, Pro, and Free users can buy directly from U.S. Etsy sellers through ChatGPT, with over 1 million Shopify merchants, like Glossier, SKIMS, Spanx and Vuori, coming soon.
Instant Checkout: Instant Checkout makes it easy to go from chat to checkout in just a few taps.
Instant Checkout: Ask ChatGPT a shopping question and it finds organic, unsponsored product results from across the web.
Instant Checkout: Choose an item and ChatGPT shows where to buy, ranking merchants by factors like inventory, price, quality, primary seller status, and Instant Checkout availability.
Instant Checkout: If Instant Checkout is offered, tap Buy, confirm shipping and payment details, and pay in one tap with your card on file or other express pay options.
Instant Checkout: If Instant Checkout isn’t available, ChatGPT provides a direct link so you can purchase from the merchant’s site.
Instant Checkout: You’re in control at all times and give explicit confirmation to purchase. Payment goes directly from you to the merchant, not through OpenAI.Learn more about Instant Checkout.
Agentic Commerce Protocol: The Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP) gives merchants and developers a way to build agentic commerce experiences that work with their current systems and connect them with 700M+ weekly active users on ChatGPT.
Agentic Commerce Protocol: If you’re a merchant and want to add Instant Checkout in ChatGPT, apply on ourmerchant page.We’ll be accepting and onboarding merchants on a rolling basis.
Agentic Commerce Protocol: Developers can start exploring the Agentic Commerce Protocoldocumentationand example implementations onGitHub.
title_updated: Instant Checkout: Launch in-chat purchasing with Etsy via Agentic Commerce Protocol
content_updated:
OpenAI introduces Instant Checkout in ChatGPT powered by the Agentic Commerce Protocol built with Stripe, enabling users to find products and complete purchases directly in chat starting with US Etsy sellers.
Instant Checkout ranks merchants by inventory, price, quality, and primary seller status, with payment going directly from user to merchant. Plus, Pro, and Free users in the US can use the feature, while over one million Shopify merchants are coming soon.
The Agentic Commerce Protocol provides merchants and developers with tools to build agentic commerce experiences connecting to ChatGPT's 700 million weekly active users, with documentation and example implementations available on GitHub.

title: Introducing ChatGPT Pulse for Pro users
content:
We’re launching an early preview of ChatGPT pulse forPro users on iOS and Android. This is a new experience where ChatGPT can now proactively deliver personalized, focused updates for you once a day. These updates are based on asynchronous research ChatGPT can do on your  behalf overnight, synthesizing information from your memory, chat history, and direct feedback to learn what’s most relevant to you.
Updates are delivered once a day as visual summaries you can scan at a glance or dive deeper into. Each topic opens into more detail where you can ask follow-up questions. You can shape what shows up in pulse with a quick thumbs up or down, or by using curate to tell ChatGPT what you’d like to see.
If you ask for something new in the evening, we aim to include it the next morning, though exact timing may vary. Pulse refreshes daily, and you can save any item into a chat to keep it.
As a preview, pulse has some limitations. It aims to show you what’s most relevant and useful but you may still see suggestions that miss the mark. For example, you may get tips for a project you already completed. You can guide what shows up by telling ChatGPT directly. It remembers your feedback for next time and improves as it learns from real use.
It requires saved memory and chat history to be turned on, and you can switch pulse off anytime in settings. SeeChatGPT Pulse.
title_updated: ChatGPT Pulse: Launch proactive daily research summaries for Pro users on iOS & Android
content_updated:
ChatGPT launches Pulse as an early preview for Pro users on iOS and Android, delivering personalized daily updates based on asynchronous research that synthesizes information from memory, chat history, and direct feedback.
Updates are delivered as visual summaries users can scan, expand, or dive deeper into with follow-up questions, with feedback via thumbs up or down and a curate feature to shape future content. New requests made in the evening aim to appear the next morning.
As a preview, Pulse has limitations and may occasionally show irrelevant suggestions, but it improves as it learns from real use. It requires saved memory and chat history to be enabled and can be switched off anytime in settings.

title: Voice Updates
content: We’re improving the quality and latency of responses of the version of Advanced Voice powered by GPT-4o mini.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Improve quality & latency for GPT-4o mini-powered Advanced Voice
content_updated:
OpenAI improves the quality and reduces the latency of responses in the version of Advanced Voice Mode powered by GPT-4o mini, making voice interactions faster and more responsive.
The update targets the GPT-4o mini voice backend specifically, which serves as a lighter-weight option within the Advanced Voice experience.
This is part of ongoing incremental improvements to Voice Mode performance following the broader Advanced Voice upgrades announced in June 2025.

title: Toggle thinking time in the message composer
content:
What’s new: We’re giving Plus, Business, and Pro users more control over how long GPT-5 thinks before responding. You can now choose the level of reasoning time, so you can decide whether you want a quicker answer or more in-depth reasoning for your task.
How it works: When you selectGPT-5 with Thinkingin ChatGPT, you’ll see a newthinking timetoggle in the message composer.
How it works: Standard(new default, balancing speed and intelligence) andExtended(the previous default for Plus) are available to allPlus, Businessusers
How it works: Prousers get two additional options:Light(snappiest) andHeavy(deeper reasoning).
How it works: All queries default toStandardunless you choose to change it. Once you choose a different thinking time, your preference is saved and used for future queries until you switch again.
Why we’re making this change: We’ve heard from some of you that GPT-5 Thinking’s answers can take longer than you’d like. Now you can decide what you need in the moment:
Why we’re making this change: Light / Standard→ quicker answers that still apply reasoning, balancing speed and intelligence.
Why we’re making this change: Extended / Heavy→ more time for deeper, more comprehensive responses when the stakes are higher or the question is more difficult.
What’s next: The thinking-time toggle is live today on chatgpt.com. Settings you choose on the web won’t yet sync to mobile, but we’ll bring this control to our iOS and Android apps in the coming weeks.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch thinking time toggle for Plus, Business & Pro users
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces a thinking time toggle in the message composer for GPT-5 with Thinking, giving users control over reasoning depth with Standard and Extended options for Plus and Business users, plus Light and Heavy options for Pro users.
Standard is the new default balancing speed and intelligence, with Extended providing the previous default reasoning depth. All preferences are saved for future queries, and the toggle is available on chatgpt.com with mobile support coming soon.
This addresses user feedback that GPT-5 Thinking answers sometimes take longer than desired, letting users decide between quicker answers and deeper reasoning based on question complexity.

title: Improvements to search in ChatGPT
content:
We’ve made further improvements to search in ChatGPT, making results more accurate, reliable, and useful.
Factuality:Fewer hallucinations, improving answer quality.
Shopping:Search is better at detecting shopping intent, showing products when you want them and keeping results focused when you don’t.
Formatting: Answers are better formatted for quick understanding without losing detail or quality.
title_updated: ChatGPT Search: Improve factuality, shopping detection & answer formatting
content_updated:
ChatGPT Search receives further improvements making results more accurate, reliable, and useful, with fewer hallucinations improving overall answer quality.
Shopping intent detection is improved so products appear when wanted and results stay focused otherwise, while answer formatting is enhanced for quick understanding without losing detail or quality.
These search improvements build on ChatGPT's ongoing effort to make its web search capabilities competitive with dedicated search engines for everyday information needs.

title: Personalization settings are now in Settings
content: Personalization no longer opens in a separate modal. You can manage personalization, choose a ChatGPT personality, add custom instructions, and update Memory directly inSettings → Personalization.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Move personalization settings from modal into main Settings pane
content_updated:
Personalization settings no longer open in a separate modal and are now accessible directly within Settings under the Personalization section, including personality selection, custom instructions, and Memory management.
This UI change consolidates personalization controls into the main settings flow, making them more discoverable and consistent with other ChatGPT configuration options.
The move simplifies the settings experience by eliminating the separate modal that previously handled personalization, reducing the number of different interfaces users need to navigate.

Use GPT-5-codex for coding-focused work in Codex, or Codex-like environments; use GPT-5 for general, non-coding tasks. Please review theannouncementblog for more information.

title_updated: Codex: Launch GPT-5-Codex model optimized for agentic coding tasks
content_updated:
OpenAI introduces GPT-5-Codex, a GPT-5 variant optimized for agentic coding, available as the default for cloud tasks and code review in Codex and selectable for local workflows via the Codex CLI and IDE extension.
GPT-5-Codex is designed for coding-focused work in Codex environments while GPT-5 remains recommended for general non-coding tasks. The model is not currently supported in ChatGPT or the API.
This specialized model represents OpenAI's investment in purpose-built coding models for the Codex platform, separate from the general-purpose GPT-5 series available in ChatGPT.

title: ChatGPT Voice Update
content:
Last month we announced that everyone now has access to Advanced Voice Mode, with usage expanded from minutes per day to hours for Free users, and near-unlimited use for Plus.
We also shared that Standard Voice Mode would be retired following a 30-day sunset period. We’ve heard feedback that Standard Voice is special to some folks, and we want to make sure the transition feels right. To that end, we’re keeping Standard Voice available while we address some feedback from its users about Advanced Voice.
Stay tuned—more improvements to Voice are coming soon.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Delay Standard Voice retirement & keep it available
content_updated:
OpenAI postpones the retirement of Standard Voice Mode, originally planned after a 30-day sunset following the August 2025 Advanced Voice expansion, keeping it available while addressing user feedback about Advanced Voice.
The decision responds to feedback that Standard Voice is valued by some users, and OpenAI wants to ensure the transition feels right before proceeding with deprecation.
Advanced Voice was recently expanded from minutes per day to hours for Free users and near-unlimited for Plus users, but the quality gap between Standard and Advanced Voice prompted this pause.

title: Branch conversations on web
content:
You can now branch conversations in ChatGPT, making it easier to explore different directions without losing your original thread. Hover over a message, clickMore actions(⋯), and selectBranch in new chatto start a separate conversation from that point.
This feature is available today for all logged-in users on web.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch conversation branching on web for all logged-in users
content_updated:
ChatGPT now supports branching conversations on web, allowing users to explore different directions from any message without losing the original thread by hovering over a message and selecting Branch in new chat.
The feature creates a separate conversation starting from the selected message point, preserving the original chat while enabling parallel exploration of alternative approaches or topics.
Available today for all logged-in web users, this addresses a common workflow need where users want to try different approaches from the same starting point without duplicating or losing their original conversation.

title: Projects Now Available on ChatGPT Free Tier
content:
Projects bring your chats and files into one place, helping you stay focused and get things done. They’re now available on our Free tier, with up to 5 file uploads per project.
We’ve also updated file uploads on paid tiers: Plus, Go, and Edu support up to 25 files per project. Pro, Business, and Enterprise support up to 40 files per project.
New customization options are available for all plans, including colors and icons, to help keep your projects organized.
Learn more aboutprojects in ChatGPT.
title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Expand to Free tier with up to 5 file uploads per project
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects are now available on the Free tier with up to 5 file uploads per project, while paid tiers receive updated limits: Plus, Go, and Edu support 25 files, and Pro, Business, and Enterprise support 40 files per project.
New customization options including colors and icons are available for all plans to help keep projects organized and visually distinguishable.
This expansion makes Projects accessible to all ChatGPT users for the first time, following the initial launch for paid users, with the Free tier receiving a limited but functional version of the organizational feature.

title: Updates to Codex (Plus/Pro)
content:
Starting today, Codex works with you everywhere you build—in your terminal or IDE, on the web, in GitHub, and even from the ChatGPT iOS app. Your ChatGPT account connects it all, so you can work seamlessly between your local environment and Codex’s cloud without losing state.
We’re excited to introduce the latest Codex updates:
IDE Extension:The new extension brings codex into VS Code, Cursor, and other VS Code forks, so that you can seamlessly preview local changes and edit code
Sign in with ChatGPT:Available in both the IDE and CLI, eliminating API key setup and providing access directly through your existing ChatGPT plan
Seamless Local ↔ Cloud Handoff:Developers can pair with Codex locally and then delegate tasks to the cloud to execute asynchronously without losing state
Upgraded Codex CLI:Refreshed UI, new commands, and bug fixes
Code reviews in GitHub:Set up Codex to automatically review new PRs in a repo, or mention @codex in PRs to get reviews and suggested fixes
Additionally, all product information and updates for Codex moving forward will be announced on our new site:developers.openai.com/codex.
We invite you to explore the site for more details on these new features, as well as guides on how to get started.
To learn more about Codex, visit the newdevelopers siteas well as our general help article:Using Codex with your ChatGPT plan.
title_updated: Codex: Launch IDE extension, ChatGPT sign-in & local-cloud handoff
content_updated:
Codex expands to work everywhere developers build with a new VS Code/Cursor IDE extension, ChatGPT sign-in eliminating API key setup, seamless local-to-cloud handoff, an upgraded CLI with refreshed UI, and automated GitHub PR code reviews.
The IDE extension brings Codex into VS Code, Cursor, and other VS Code forks for previewing local changes and editing code, while the local-to-cloud handoff lets developers pair locally then delegate tasks to the cloud asynchronously.
All Codex product information and updates are moving to a new dedicated developers site at developers.openai.com/codex, signaling the maturation of Codex from a feature into a full developer platform.

title: Project-only memory
content:
An improvement toprojectsis now available. When creating a project, users have the option to enableproject-only memory.
Withproject-only memoryenabled, ChatGPT can use other conversations in that project for additional context, and won’t use yoursaved memoriesfrom outside the project to shape responses. Additionally, it won’t carry anything from the project into future chats outside of the project.
This creates a focused, self-contained space, which is useful for long-running or sensitive work where you want ChatGPT to stay anchored to that project’s tone, context, and history.
Note:
Personal Memorymust be enabled to utilize this feature.Settings->Personalization->Memory
This feature will initially only be available on the ChatGPT website and Windows app. Support for mobile (iOS and Android) and macOS app will follow in the coming weeks.
Learn more aboutmemory in projects.
title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Launch project-only memory for focused, self-contained workspaces
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects now offer a project-only memory option that creates a focused, self-contained workspace where ChatGPT uses only conversations within that project for context and does not reference or carry information to or from outside the project.
Personal Memory must be enabled to use this feature, which is initially available on the ChatGPT website and Windows app, with iOS, Android, and macOS app support following in coming weeks.
This feature is useful for long-running or sensitive work where users want ChatGPT anchored to a specific project's tone, context, and history without cross-contamination from other conversations.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Launch low-cost subscription in India at Rs 399/month
content_updated:

title: GPT-5 Updates
content:
We’re making GPT-5’s default personality warmer and more familiar. This is in response to user feedback that the initial version of GPT-5 came across as too reserved and professional. The differences in personality should feel subtle but create a noticeably more approachable ChatGPT experience.
Warmth here means small acknowledgements that make interactions feel more personable — for example, “Good question,” “Great start,” or briefly recognizing the user’s circumstances when relevant. This is different from sycophancy, which we define as excessive flattery that can feel insincere. You can find more about our learnings on reducing sycophancyhere.
This update does not show an increase in sycophancy compared to the previous personality of GPT-5, measured by the same internal evals we ran and detailed in oursystem card. Teaching models to be warm without being sycophantic is an ongoing research challenge, and we will continue iterating to get this right. More updates are coming soon.
title_updated: GPT-5: Update default personality to be warmer & more approachable
content_updated:
OpenAI makes GPT-5's default personality warmer and more familiar in response to user feedback that the initial version came across as too reserved and professional, with small acknowledgements like brief recognitions of user circumstances.
The warmth improvements are distinct from sycophancy, defined as excessive flattery, and internal evaluations confirm no increase in sycophantic behavior compared to the previous GPT-5 personality.
Teaching models to be warm without being sycophantic remains an ongoing research challenge, with this update representing OpenAI's first personality adjustment to GPT-5 since its August 2025 launch.

title: Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts Connectors in ChatGPT (Plus)
content:
Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts Connectors in ChatGPT are now available to Plus users globally. Access will be granted to users throughout today.
This was previously made available to Pro users onAugust 12, 2025.
Learn more aboutconnectors.
title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Expand Gmail, Google Calendar & Contacts to Plus users
content_updated:
Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts connectors are now available to ChatGPT Plus users globally, expanding access that was previously limited to Pro users since August 12, 2025.
Once enabled, ChatGPT automatically references these connectors when relevant, making it faster to bring Google workspace information into conversations without manual selection each time.

title: GPT-5 Updates
content:
You can now choose between “Auto”, “Fast”, and “Thinking” for GPT-5. Most users will want Auto, but the additional control will be useful for some people.
ChatGPT Plus users now have 3,000 messages/week with GPT-5 Thinking, and then extra capacity on GPT-5 Thinking mini after that limit. The context limit for GPT-5 Thinking is 196k tokens. We may have to update rate limits over time depending on usage.
4o is back in the model picker for all paid users by default. Paid users also now have a “Show additional models” toggle in ChatGPT web settings which will add models like o3, o4-mini, 4.1, and GPT-5 Thinking mini. 4.5 is only available to Pro users due to GPUs.
title_updated: GPT-5: Add Auto, Fast & Thinking model selector with expanded rate limits
content_updated:
ChatGPT now offers Auto, Fast, and Thinking options for GPT-5, with Plus users receiving 3,000 messages per week with GPT-5 Thinking and extra capacity on GPT-5 Thinking mini after that limit, with a 196k token context.
GPT-4o returns to the model picker for all paid users by default, with a Show additional models toggle in web settings adding o3, o4-mini, 4.1, and GPT-5 Thinking mini, while GPT-4.5 remains Pro-exclusive due to GPU constraints.
This update addresses user demand for more granular model control, moving away from the initial auto-only GPT-5 experience toward explicit selection options.

title: Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts Connectors in ChatGPT (Pro)
content:
Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts are now available to connect and use in chat. Once you enable them, ChatGPT will automatically reference them when relevant, making it faster and easier to bring information from these tools into your conversations without having to manually select them each time.
This capability is part of GPT-5 and will begin rolling out to Pro users globally this week, followed by Plus, Team, Enterprise, and Edu plans in the coming weeks. To enable, visitSettings→Connectors→Connecton the application.
If you already have Gmail or Google Calendar enabled for deep research, you can now also use them in chat. To use them in deep research, you will still need to enable each connector separately and select it every time you start a new deep research request.
Learn more aboutconnectors.
title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Launch Gmail, Google Calendar & Contacts for Pro users
content_updated:
Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts are now available as connectors in ChatGPT, automatically referencing relevant information from these tools in conversations once enabled by the user.
Rolling out to Pro users globally first, with Plus, Team, Enterprise, and Edu plans following in coming weeks. Users who already have Gmail or Google Calendar enabled for deep research can now also use them in chat.
This represents a significant expansion of ChatGPT's integration capabilities as part of GPT-5, enabling seamless access to personal productivity data without manually selecting connectors for each conversation.

title: Additional connectors for Plus and Pro
content:
Now connect even more tools in ChatGPT for useful, relevant responses in chat.
The following connectors are now available to search by chat in addition to deep research.
Plus:Box, Canva, Dropbox, HubSpot, Notion, Microsoft SharePoint, and Microsoft Teams
Pro: Microsoft Teams and Github

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Expand Box, Canva, Dropbox, HubSpot & more to Plus & Pro chat
content_updated:
ChatGPT expands connector availability for chat search, adding Box, Canva, Dropbox, HubSpot, Notion, Microsoft SharePoint, and Microsoft Teams for Plus users, and Microsoft Teams and GitHub for Pro users.
Rollout begins immediately with all eligible accounts gaining access in coming days. Connectors are enabled via Settings and remain in beta, defaulted off for Enterprise and Edu plans where admins can enable them.
Connectors for Plus and Pro plans are not available in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK, with this expansion building on the deep research connectors that were previously the only way to access third-party data.

title: Image generation update to ChatGPT Free
content: We're rolling out a small update to image generation for ChatGPT Free tier users that makes its performance more efficient.
title_updated: ChatGPT Images: Improve generation efficiency for Free tier users
content_updated:
OpenAI rolls out an update to image generation for ChatGPT Free tier users that makes performance more efficient, optimizing how images are generated without changing the user-facing feature set.
The update targets the backend efficiency of image generation specifically for free-tier users, likely reducing compute costs while maintaining output quality.
This efficiency improvement allows OpenAI to sustain image generation availability for free users as demand grows, following the broader ChatGPT Images launch earlier in the year.

title: GPT-5
content:
GPT-5 is slowly rolling out to all users on ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Team, and Free plans worldwide across web, mobile, and desktop. GPT-5 will be available to ChatGPT Enterprise and Edu plans soon.
GPT-5 in ChatGPT is our next flagship model and the new default for all logged-in users. It simplifies ChatGPT to a single auto-switching system that brings together the best of our previous models into asmart, fast model.
GPT-5 is available to all ChatGPT Tiers. Users on Paid tiers - Plus, Pro, and Team - have access to the model picker, which enables you to manually select GPT-5 or GPT-5 Thinking. Pro and Team tier users have access to GPT-5 Thinking Pro, which takes a bit longer to think but delivers the accuracy you need for complex tasks.
Learn more about GPT-5 in ChatGPT.
title_updated: GPT-5: Launch flagship auto-switching model for all ChatGPT users worldwide
content_updated:
OpenAI launches GPT-5 in ChatGPT as the new default flagship model rolling out to Plus, Pro, Team, and Free plans worldwide across web, mobile, and desktop, with Enterprise and Edu plans following soon.
GPT-5 simplifies ChatGPT to a single auto-switching system combining the best of previous models, with paid users accessing the model picker for manual GPT-5 or GPT-5 Thinking selection, and Pro and Team users getting GPT-5 Thinking Pro for complex tasks.
GPT-5 represents a fundamental shift from multiple model choices to a unified intelligent system that automatically selects the best approach for each query.

title: Personalities
content:
You can now choose from four distinct personalities or use the Default personality in yourCustomize ChatGPT settings. Default is the standard ChatGPT style: clear, neutral, and adaptable. The other personalities each have their own style and tone, described below.
Cynic– Sarcastic and dry, delivers blunt help with wit. Often teases, but provides direct, practical answers when it matters.
Robot– Precise, efficient, and emotionless, delivering direct answers without extra words.
Listener– Warm and laid-back, reflecting your thoughts back with calm clarity and light wit.
Nerd– Playful and curious, explaining concepts clearly while celebrating knowledge and discovery.
Please note that these personalities will not apply to Voice mode.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch four personality options plus Default style
content_updated:
ChatGPT now offers four distinct personality options alongside the Default style in Customize settings: Cynic for sarcastic directness, Robot for precise efficiency, Listener for warm reflection, and Nerd for playful curiosity.
Each personality has its own communication style and tone that applies to text conversations, though personalities do not apply to Voice mode at launch.
This marks ChatGPT's first personality system, giving users the ability to choose how ChatGPT communicates beyond custom instructions, launched alongside the GPT-5 rollout.

title: Accent Colors
content:
You can now set an accent color that applies to elements in ChatGPT, including your conversation bubbles, the Voice button, and highlighted text.
On web:Click your profile icon at the bottom left, selectSettings, go to theGeneraltab, and choose an option from theAccent colordrop-down.
On mobile (iOS and Android):Tap your profile icon at the bottom, go toPersonalization, then selectColor Schemeto pick your accent color.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch accent color customization for conversation UI
content_updated:
ChatGPT now lets users set an accent color that applies to conversation bubbles, the Voice button, highlighted text, and other UI elements across the interface.
On web, the setting is accessible via profile icon, Settings, General tab, and Accent color dropdown. On mobile, users navigate to profile, Personalization, then Color Scheme to select their preferred accent.
This visual customization option launches alongside GPT-5 and the personality system, expanding user personalization beyond conversation style to include the visual appearance of the interface.

title: ChatGPT Voice updates
content:
Today we’re rolling out improvements to Voice Mode to make it more accessible and useful for everyone. It also now works with custom GPTs. We’re expanding access with near-unlimited use for Plus users and hours each day for Free users. To simplify the experience, Standard Voice Mode will be retired in 30 days, unifying all users onto our latest voice experience.
For paid users, Voice now adapts to your instructions, adjusting its speaking style (length, speed, tone, and more) to fit the moment.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Expand Advanced Voice access & announce Standard Voice retirement
content_updated:
ChatGPT expands Advanced Voice Mode with near-unlimited use for Plus users and hours per day for Free users, now supporting custom GPTs and paid-user instruction adaptation for style, speed, and tone.
Voice now adapts to user instructions for paid users, and Standard Voice Mode will be retired in 30 days to unify all users onto the latest voice experience.
This represents the most significant expansion of Voice Mode accessibility since its initial launch, moving from limited daily minutes to extensive daily hours while simplifying the product by phasing out the legacy Standard Voice.

title: Study Mode
content:
Study mode is currently available to users in Free, Plus, Pro, and Teams plans globally and will expand to Edu plans in the coming weeks. Study mode works with any model available in ChatGPT on iOS, Android, web, and desktop.
Study mode is a new learning experience in ChatGPT designed to help you build a deeper understanding of any topic. When you turn it on, ChatGPT will ask interactive questions to understand your goals and skill level, then work with you to reach the answer together.
With Study mode enabled, ChatGPT can:
Guide understanding with Socratic-style questions.
Break concepts into easy to follow sections starting simple and adding complexity as you progress.
Personalize responses based on your past chats if memory is on, using examples and tips tailored to you.
Check your understanding with open-ended prompts and feedback.
Work with your materials by referencing images or PDFs you upload.
You can enable study mode at any time by selectingToolsin the prompt window and choosingStudy and learnfrom the drop‑down menu or go tochatgpt.com/studymode.
Study mode is powered by custom system instructions and can have some inconsistent behavior and mistakes across conversations. We plan on training this behavior directly into our main models once we’ve learned what works best through iteration and user feedback.
Learn more about study mode.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch Study Mode for interactive Socratic-style learning
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces Study Mode, a learning experience that uses interactive Socratic-style questions to understand goals and skill level, then guides users to answers through progressive concept building and personalized feedback.
Available on Free, Plus, Pro, and Teams plans globally across iOS, Android, web, and desktop, Study Mode can be enabled via the Tools menu or chatgpt.com/studymode, and works with uploaded materials like images and PDFs.
Study Mode is powered by custom system instructions and may have inconsistent behavior across conversations, with plans to train the behavior directly into main models once iteration and user feedback reveal what works best.

title: Canva and Notion connectors (Pro)
content:
Pro users can now connect to Canva and Notion for both chat search and deep research.
Note this feature is currently limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
Learn more aboutconnectors.
title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Add Canva & Notion for Pro users in chat & deep research
content_updated:
Pro users can now connect Canva and Notion for both chat search and deep research, expanding the connector ecosystem with two popular creative and productivity tools.
This feature is currently limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK due to regional regulatory considerations.
The addition continues the expansion of Pro-exclusive connector integrations, building on earlier Google workspace and Microsoft connectors to cover more of the tools knowledge workers use daily.

title: Chat search for HubSpot and custom connectors (MCP) (Pro)
content:
Pro users can now utilize chat search with HubSpot and custom connectors (MCP) in addition to deep research.
Note this feature is currently limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
Learn more aboutconnectors, as well ashow to create a custom connector using MCP.
title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Add HubSpot & custom MCP connectors for Pro chat search
content_updated:
Pro users can now use chat search with HubSpot and custom connectors built via Model Context Protocol in addition to their existing deep research functionality.
This feature is limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK, matching the regional restrictions of other connector features.
The MCP custom connector support in chat search is particularly significant, allowing Pro users to connect proprietary systems to ChatGPT's conversational interface beyond the pre-built integrations.

title: Updates to Advanced Voice Mode for Free tier users
content: The Advanced Voice upgradeswe announced on June 7thare now rolling out to ChatGPT free users too. With the same improvements available to paid users, ChatGPT sounds more natural and expressive, and can translate more effectively. Rate limits for free users stay the same.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Roll out Advanced Voice improvements to Free tier users
content_updated:
The Advanced Voice upgrades announced on June 7 for paid users are now rolling out to ChatGPT Free users, delivering the same improvements in natural-sounding speech, expressiveness, and translation effectiveness.
Rate limits for Free users remain unchanged despite the quality improvements, maintaining the existing usage boundaries while upgrading the underlying voice experience.
This brings Free users to parity with paid users on Advanced Voice quality, approximately six weeks after the initial paid-user rollout of enhanced intonation, cadence, and emotion handling.

title: Record mode in ChatGPT macOS desktop app (Plus)
content:
Record mode is now available to ChatGPT Plus users globally in the macOS desktop app.
Record live conversations like team meetings or voice notes, and turn those into an editable summary in canvas.
Available on the macOS desktop app only.
Learn more aboutrecord mode.
Note: Record mode originally rolled out to Team users on June 4, and Enterprise & Edu plans on June 18.
title_updated: ChatGPT macOS: Launch Record Mode for meeting transcription & summarization for Plus users
content_updated:
Record Mode is now available to ChatGPT Plus users globally in the macOS desktop app, allowing users to record live conversations like team meetings or voice notes and turn them into editable summaries in Canvas.
The feature is exclusive to the macOS desktop app and produces summaries that can be further edited and refined using Canvas's collaborative editing capabilities.
Record Mode originally rolled out to Team users on June 4 and Enterprise and Edu plans on June 18, with this Plus expansion completing the rollout across all paid tiers.

title: Chat search connectors (Pro)
content:
Pro users are now able to use chat search connectors (in addition to deep research connectors) for the following integrations: Dropbox, Box, Google Drive (synced and non-synced), Microsoft OneDrive (Business), Microsoft SharePoint.
Note this feature is currently limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
Learn more aboutConnectors.
title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Launch chat search for Dropbox, Box, Google Drive & more for Pro users
content_updated:
Pro users can now use chat search connectors for Dropbox, Box, Google Drive (synced and non-synced), Microsoft OneDrive Business, and Microsoft SharePoint, in addition to existing deep research connector support.
This feature is limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK, and can be enabled from Connector settings in the application.
The expansion from deep-research-only to chat search means Pro users can now reference files from these cloud storage services in everyday conversations, not just during formal research sessions.

title: Project file limit increased (Pro)
content: Projects can now support 40 uploaded files, up from 20, for Pro users.
title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Increase file upload limit to 40 for Pro users
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects now support up to 40 uploaded files per project for Pro users, doubled from the previous limit of 20 files.
This increase applies to Pro users only and enables larger knowledge bases within individual projects for more comprehensive context in conversations.
The expanded limit supports power users who rely on Projects for document-heavy workflows like research, analysis, and content creation that require extensive reference material.

title: ChatGPT record mode
content:
Capture meetings, brainstorms, or voice notes. Launching today for Pro, Enterprise, and Edu users. Previously launched for Team users on June 4, 2025.
ChatGPT will transcribe, summarize, and turn them into helpful outputs like follow-ups, plans, or even code.
Available on the macOS desktop app only.
Learn more aboutChatGPT Record.
title_updated: ChatGPT Record: Launch meeting capture for Pro, Enterprise & Edu on macOS
content_updated:
ChatGPT Record Mode launches for Pro, Enterprise, and Edu users on the macOS desktop app, enabling capture of meetings, brainstorms, and voice notes with automatic transcription, summarization, and output generation.
The feature can produce follow-ups, plans, and even code from recorded audio, available exclusively on the macOS desktop app. Team users received access on June 4, 2025.
Record Mode represents ChatGPT's first ambient audio feature, extending the product beyond interactive chat into passive capture and processing of real-world conversations.

title: Expanded Model Support for Custom GPTs
content:
Enterprise and Edu users can also now choose from the full set of ChatGPT models (GPT-4o, o3, o4-mini and more) when building Custom GPTs. This was launched to Plus, Pro and Team users earlier this month.
Key details:
GPTswithout Custom Actionscan use the model picker to select from all models available to the user.
GPTswith Custom Actionscurrently support GPT-4o and 4.1
Available onwebfor users on all paid plans (Plus, Pro Team, Enterprise, Edu).
title_updated: Custom GPTs: Expand model selection to Enterprise & Edu users
content_updated:
Enterprise and Edu users can now choose from the full set of ChatGPT models including GPT-4o, o3, o4-mini, and more when building Custom GPTs, matching the model selection previously available to Plus, Pro, and Team users.
GPTs without Custom Actions can use the model picker to select from all available models, while GPTs with Custom Actions currently support only GPT-4o and GPT-4.1.
This expansion completes the rollout of multi-model Custom GPT support across all paid tiers, available on web for all paid plans.
