title: Improvements to the ChatGPT search response quality
content:
We’ve upgraded ChatGPT search for all users to provide even more comprehensive, up-to-date responses. In testing, we found users preferred these search improvements over our previous search experience.
Improved quality: Smarter responses that are more intelligent, are better at understanding what you’re asking, and provide more comprehensive answers.
Improved quality: Handles longer conversational contexts, allowing better intelligence in longer conversations.
Improved search capability and instruction following: More robust ability to follow instructions, especially in longer conversations, significantly reducing repetitive responses.
Improved search capability and instruction following: Capability to run multiple searches automatically for complex or difficult questions.
Improved search capability and instruction following: Search the web using an image you’ve uploaded.
Known limitations: Users may notice longer responses with this new search experience.
Known limitations: In some cases, a “chain of thought” reasoning will show up unexpectedly for simple queries. A fix for this is rolling out to users shortly.
Known limitations: ChatGPT may still make occasional mistakes - please double check responses.
title_updated: ChatGPT Search: Upgrade with multi-search, image search & instruction following
content_updated:
ChatGPT Search is upgraded for all users with smarter responses, improved understanding of queries, more comprehensive answers, and better handling of longer conversational contexts.
New capabilities include automatic multi-search for complex questions, image-based web search from uploaded photos, and more robust instruction following especially in longer conversations that significantly reduces repetitive responses.
Users may notice longer responses with this experience, and occasional unexpected chain-of-thought reasoning may appear for simple queries, with a fix rolling out shortly.

title: Expanded Model Support for Custom GPTs
content:
Creators can now choose from the full set of ChatGPT models (GPT-4o, o3, o4-mini and more) when building Custom GPTs—making it easier to fine-tune performance for different tasks, industries, and workflows. Creators can also set a recommended model to guide users.
Key details:
GPTswithout Custom Actionscan use the model picker to select from all models available to the user.
GPTswith Custom Actionscurrently support GPT-4o and 4.1
Available onwebfor users onPlus, Pro and Teamplans.
Enterprise and Edu rollout coming soon.
title_updated: Custom GPTs: Launch multi-model selection for Plus, Pro & Team creators
content_updated:
Custom GPT creators on Plus, Pro, and Team plans can now choose from the full set of ChatGPT models including GPT-4o, o3, o4-mini, and more when building GPTs, with the ability to set a recommended model for users.
GPTs without Custom Actions support the full model picker, while GPTs with Custom Actions currently support GPT-4o and GPT-4.1 only, available on web.
Enterprise and Edu rollout is coming soon, expanding creator control over which model powers their custom GPT for different tasks and workflows.

title: Adding More Capabilities to Projects
content:
Starting today, we’re adding several updates to projects in ChatGPT to help you do more focused work. These updates are available for Plus, Pro, and Team users.
Deep research and voice mode support
Improvements to memory to reference past chats in a project*
Sharing chats from projects
Starting a new project directly from a chat
Upload files and access model selector on mobile
Learn moreabout projects.
*Memory improvements are available for Plus and Pro users.
title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Add deep research, voice mode, memory & mobile file uploads
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects receive several updates for Plus, Pro, and Team users, including deep research and voice mode support within projects, improvements to memory for referencing past project chats, and the ability to share chats from projects.
Additional updates include starting new projects directly from a chat, uploading files on mobile, and accessing the model selector on mobile. Memory improvements are limited to Plus and Pro users.
This update transforms Projects from a basic organizational tool into a more capable workspace with access to ChatGPT's most powerful features directly within the project context.

title: Today, we're launching OpenAI o3-pro—available now for Pro users in ChatGPT and in our API.
content:
Like o1-pro, o3-pro is a version of our most intelligent model, o3, designed to think longer and provide the most reliable responses. Since the launch of o1-pro, users have favored this model for domains such as math, science, and coding—areas where o3-pro continues to excel, as shown in academic evaluations. Like o3, o3-pro has access to tools that make ChatGPT useful—it can search the web, analyze files, reason about visual inputs, use Python, personalize responses using memory, and more. Because o3-pro has access to tools, responses typically take longer than o1-pro to complete. We recommend using it for challenging questions where reliability matters more than speed, and waiting a few minutes is worth the tradeoff.
In expert evaluations, reviewers consistently prefer o3-pro over o3 in every tested category and especially in key domains like science, education, programming, business, and writing help. Reviewers also rated o3-pro consistently higher for clarity, comprehensiveness, instruction-following, and accuracy.
Academic evaluations show that o3-pro consistently outperforms both o1-pro and o3.
To assess the key strength of o3-pro, we once again use our rigorous "4/4 reliability" evaluation, where a model is considered successful only if it correctly answers a question in all four attempts, not just one:
o3-pro is available in the model picker for Pro and Team users starting today, replacing o1-pro. Enterprise and Edu users will get access the week after.
As o3-pro uses the same underlying model as o3, full safety details can be found in theo3 system card.
Limitations
At the moment, temporary chats are disabled for o3-pro as we resolve a technical issue.
Image generation is not supported within o3-pro—please use GPT-4o, OpenAI o3, or OpenAI o4-mini to generate images.
Canvas is also currently not supported within o3-pro.
title_updated: o3-pro: Launch highest-reliability reasoning model for Pro & Team users
content_updated:
OpenAI launches o3-pro for Pro and Team users in ChatGPT, a version of the o3 model designed to think longer and provide the most reliable responses, replacing o1-pro in the model picker with Enterprise and Edu access following.
In expert evaluations, o3-pro is consistently preferred over o3 in every tested category and outperforms both o1-pro and o3 on academic benchmarks using rigorous 4/4 reliability scoring where all four attempts must be correct.
Unlike o1-pro, o3-pro has access to all ChatGPT tools including web search, file analysis, Python, memory, and visual reasoning, though responses typically take longer and temporary chats, image generation, and Canvas are not currently supported.

title: Updates to Advanced Voice Mode for paid users
content:
We're upgrading Advanced Voice in ChatGPT for paid users with significant enhancements in intonation and naturalness, making interactions feel more fluid and human-like. When we first launched Advanced Voice, it represented a leap forward in AI speech—now, it speaks even more naturally, with subtler intonation, realistic cadence (including pauses and emphases), and more on-point expressiveness for certain emotions including empathy, sarcasm, and more.
Voice also now offers intuitive and effective language translation. Just ask Voice to translate between languages, and it will continue translating throughout your conversation until you tell it to stop or switch. It’s ready to translate whenever you need it—whether you're asking for directions in Italy or chatting with a colleague from the Tokyo office. For example, at a restaurant in Brazil, Voice can translate your English sentences into Portuguese, and the waiter’s Portuguese responses back into English—making conversations effortless, no matter where you are or who you're speaking with.
This upgrade to Advanced Voice is available for all paid users across markets and platforms—just tap the Voice icon in the message composer to get started.
This update is in addition to improvements we made earlier this year to ensure fewer interruptions and improved accents.
Known Limitations: In testing, we've observed that this update may occasionally cause minor decreases in audio quality, including unexpected variations in tone and pitch. These issues are more noticeable with certain voice options. We expect to improve audio consistency over time.
Known Limitations: Additionally, rare hallucinations in Voice Mode persist with this update, resulting in unintended sounds resembling ads, gibberish, or background music. We are actively investigating these issues and working toward a solution.
title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Upgrade Advanced Voice with natural intonation & real-time translation
content_updated:
Advanced Voice Mode receives a major upgrade for all paid users with significantly enhanced intonation, realistic cadence with natural pauses and emphases, and more expressive emotion handling including empathy, sarcasm, and more.
Voice now offers intuitive real-time language translation that persists throughout conversations, enabling use cases like restaurant conversations abroad or multilingual business meetings with seamless back-and-forth translation.
Available across all platforms for paid users, with known limitations including occasional audio quality variations with certain voice options and rare hallucinations producing unintended sounds resembling ads, gibberish, or background music.

title: Connectors in beta for deep research (Plus, Pro, Team, Enterprise, Edu)
content:
ChatGPT Team, Enterprise, and Edu customers globally can use connectors in deep research, as well as Pro and Plus users (excluding users in Switzerland, EEA, and the UK) to generate long-form, cited responses that include your company’s internal tools.
Supported connectors: Google Drive, SharePoint, Dropbox, Box, Outlook, Gmail, Google Calendar, Linear, GitHub, HubSpot, and Teams
Combines internal + web sources for synthesis
Learn more aboutConnectors in ChatGPT.
title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Launch beta deep research integration for all paid plans
content_updated:
ChatGPT connectors launch in beta for deep research across Team, Enterprise, Edu, Pro, and Plus plans globally, enabling long-form cited responses that combine internal tools with web sources.
Supported connectors include Google Drive, SharePoint, Dropbox, Box, Outlook, Gmail, Google Calendar, Linear, GitHub, HubSpot, and Teams, with Pro and Plus users excluding those in Switzerland, EEA, and the UK.
This marks the first time ChatGPT can synthesize information from enterprise tools alongside web sources in deep research, bridging the gap between public knowledge and private organizational data.

title: Custom connectors via Model Context Protocol (Pro, Team, Enterprise, Edu)
content:
Admins and users can now build and deploy custom connectors to proprietary systems using Model Context Protocol (MCP).
Requires a remote MCP server
Available only in deep research
Admin-published connectors appear in the connector list for all users
Learn more aboutbuilding custom connectors with MCP. For Team, Enterprise, and Edu plans, only admins can build and deploy custom connectors.
title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Launch custom MCP connectors for Pro, Team, Enterprise & Edu
content_updated:
Admins and users can now build and deploy custom connectors to proprietary systems using Model Context Protocol, requiring a remote MCP server and currently available only in deep research.
Admin-published connectors appear in the connector list for all workspace users, with Team, Enterprise, and Edu plans restricting custom connector creation to admins only.
MCP custom connectors enable organizations to connect ChatGPT to internal systems beyond the pre-built integrations, opening deep research to proprietary databases, APIs, and tools specific to each organization.

title: Memory is now more comprehensive for Free users
content:
Memory improvements are starting to roll out to Free users. In addition to the saved memories that were there before, ChatGPT now references your recent conversations to deliver responses that feel more relevant and tailored to you.
Free users must be logged in and on up-to-date apps (iOS/Android v1.2025.147+).
Opt‑in reminders: Free users in EEA (EU + UK), Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, or Liechtenstein will see a prompt to enable this setting or can visitSettings>Personalization>Memory>Reference chat historyto enable.
Opt‑in reminders: Outside the European regions listed above, all Free users that have memory enabled will receive the upgrade automatically.
Opt‑in reminders: You can turn off memory anytime in settings. Learn more in ourMemory FAQ.
title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Expand comprehensive memory with chat history to Free users
content_updated:
ChatGPT's enhanced memory improvements begin rolling out to Free users, adding conversation history reference alongside existing saved memories to deliver more relevant and tailored responses.
Free users must be logged in with up-to-date apps, and users in the EEA, Switzerland, and related regions must explicitly opt in via Settings, while users elsewhere receive the upgrade automatically if memory is enabled.
This expansion brings Free users closer to the memory experience available to Plus and Pro users since May 2025, making ChatGPT's long-term personalization accessible across all tiers.

title: Dropbox connector for deep research for Plus/Pro/Team
content:
ChatGPT deep research with Dropbox is available globally to Team users. It is also gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users, except for those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK. Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date.
See:Connect apps to ChatGPT deep research
title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Add Dropbox connector for Plus, Pro & Team users
content_updated:
ChatGPT deep research now integrates with Dropbox, available globally to Team users and gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users except those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
Once connected, deep research can access and reference Dropbox files alongside web sources to generate more comprehensive and contextually relevant research reports.
Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date, continuing the phased connector rollout that started with Google Drive and SharePoint earlier in 2025.

title: GitHub connector for deep research
content: The GitHub connector is now available globally to Plus/Pro/Team users, including those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Launch GitHub connector globally for Plus, Pro & Team
content_updated:
The GitHub connector for ChatGPT deep research is now available globally to Plus, Pro, and Team users, including those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
This makes GitHub the first deep research connector available without regional restrictions, enabling developers to reference repository data directly in research reports.
The global availability contrasts with other connectors like Dropbox and SharePoint that exclude EEA and UK users, suggesting GitHub met different regulatory requirements for unrestricted deployment.

title: Releasing GPT-4.1 in ChatGPT for all paid users
content:
Since its launch in the API in April, GPT-4.1 has become a favorite among developers—by popular demand, we’re making it available directly in ChatGPT.
GPT-4.1 is a specialized model that excels at coding tasks. Compared to GPT-4o, it's even stronger at precise instruction following and web development tasks, and offers an alternative to OpenAI o3 and OpenAI o4-mini for simpler, everyday coding needs.
Starting today, Plus, Pro, and Team users can access GPT-4.1 via the "more models" dropdown in the model picker. Enterprise and Edu users will get access in the coming weeks. GPT-4.1 has the same rate limits as GPT-4o for paid users.
title_updated: GPT-4.1: Launch in ChatGPT for all paid users as specialized coding model
content_updated:
GPT-4.1, previously available only in the API since April, is now accessible in ChatGPT for Plus, Pro, and Team users via the more models dropdown, with Enterprise and Edu access coming in following weeks.
GPT-4.1 excels at coding tasks with stronger instruction following and web development capabilities compared to GPT-4o, offering an alternative to o3 and o4-mini for simpler everyday coding needs with the same rate limits as GPT-4o.
This brings a developer-favorite API model to the ChatGPT consumer product by popular demand, bridging the gap between API and chat model availability.

title: Introducing GPT-4.1 mini, replacing GPT-4o mini, in ChatGPT for all users
content:
GPT-4.1 mini is a fast, capable, and efficient small model, delivering significant improvements compared to GPT-4o mini—in instruction-following, coding, and overall intelligence. Starting today, GPT-4.1 mini replaces GPT-4o mini in the model picker under "more models" for paid users, and will serve as the fallback model for free users once they reach their GPT-4o usage limits. Rate limits remain the same.
Evals for GPT-4.1 and GPT-4.1 mini were originally shared in theblog postaccompanying their API release. They also went through standard safety evaluations. Detailed results are available in the newly launchedSafety Evaluations Hub.
title_updated: GPT-4.1 mini: Launch as GPT-4o mini replacement for all ChatGPT users
content_updated:
GPT-4.1 mini launches as a fast, capable small model replacing GPT-4o mini in the ChatGPT model picker, delivering significant improvements in instruction following, coding, and overall intelligence.
For paid users, GPT-4.1 mini appears under more models, while for free users it serves as the fallback model once GPT-4o usage limits are reached, with rate limits remaining unchanged.
Safety evaluations for both GPT-4.1 and GPT-4.1 mini are available in the newly launched Safety Evaluations Hub, providing transparency into model safety testing.

title: Microsoft Sharepoint and OneDrive connector for deep research for Plus/Pro/Team
content:
ChatGPT deep research with Sharepoint and OneDrive is available globally to Team users. It is also gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users, except for those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK. Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date.
See:Connecting SharePoint and Microsoft OneDrive to ChatGPT deep research
title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Add SharePoint & OneDrive connector for Plus, Pro & Team
content_updated:
ChatGPT deep research now integrates with Microsoft SharePoint and OneDrive, available globally to Team users and gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users except those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
The connector enables deep research to access and reference documents stored in SharePoint and OneDrive alongside web sources for more comprehensive enterprise-oriented research reports.
Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date, with this Microsoft integration complementing the existing Google Drive connector for deep research.

title: Export Deep Research as PDF for Plus/Pro/Team
content:
You can now export your deep research reports as well-formatted PDFs—complete with tables, images, linked citations, and sources.
To use, click the share icon and select 'Download as PDF.' It works for both new and past reports.
title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Launch PDF export for Plus, Pro & Team users
content_updated:
ChatGPT deep research reports can now be exported as well-formatted PDFs complete with tables, images, linked citations, and sources, available for both new and past reports.
Users click the share icon and select Download as PDF to generate the export, providing a professional-quality document suitable for sharing and archiving.
This addresses the need to share deep research findings outside of ChatGPT, enabling users to distribute reports as standalone documents with proper formatting and citation attribution.

title: GitHub connector for deep research for Plus/Pro/Team
content:
ChatGPT deep research with GitHub is available globally to Team users. It is also gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users, except for those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK. Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date.
See:Connecting GitHub to ChatGPT deep research.
title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Add GitHub connector for Plus, Pro & Team users
content_updated:
ChatGPT deep research now integrates with GitHub, available globally to Team users and gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users except those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
The connector enables deep research to access and reference GitHub repositories, issues, and code alongside web sources for more comprehensive developer-oriented research reports.
Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date, with GitHub joining Google Drive, SharePoint, and Dropbox as available deep research data sources.

title: Enhanced Memory in ChatGPT (including EU) on Plus/Pro
content:
Enhanced memory rolling out to all Plus and Pro users (including the EU). The new memory features are available in the EEA (EU + UK), Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, or Liechtenstein. These features areOFF by defaultand must be enabled inSettings > Personalization > Reference  Chat  History.
Plan differences: •Saved  memoriesandChat historyare offered only to Plus and Pro accounts.
Plan differences: • Free‑tier users have access toSaved  memoriesonly.
Opt‑in reminders: • Outside the European regions listed above, all Plus and Pro accounts that have memory enabled will receive the upgrade automatically.
Opt‑in reminders: • If you previously opted out of memory, ChatGPT will not reference past conversations unless you opt back in.
Opt‑in reminders: SeeMemory FAQ.
title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Launch enhanced memory with chat history for EU Plus & Pro users
content_updated:
Enhanced memory featuring chat history reference rolls out to all Plus and Pro users globally, including the EEA, Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein where the features are off by default and must be manually enabled.
Plus and Pro accounts get both saved memories and chat history, while Free-tier users have access to saved memories only. Users outside European regions who have memory enabled receive the upgrade automatically.
This marks a significant milestone as the first time ChatGPT's enhanced memory features are available in the EU and related regions, requiring explicit opt-in to comply with European data protection requirements.

title: Mobile UI (iOS/Android) changing on Free/Plus/Pro plans
content:
We've removed the row of individual tool icons from the mobile composer and replaces it with the new sliders‑style icon to open theSkillsmenu; tapping that button opens a bottom‑sheet menu where users can choose tools like Create an image or Search the web.
No tools are deprecated—access is simply consolidated to clear space and reduce on‑screen clutter.
title_updated: ChatGPT Mobile: Consolidate tool icons into Skills menu on iOS & Android
content_updated:
ChatGPT removes the row of individual tool icons from the mobile composer on iOS and Android, replacing them with a single sliders-style icon that opens a new Skills menu bottom sheet.
No tools are deprecated in this change; access to features like Create an image and Search the web is simply consolidated into the Skills menu to clear space and reduce on-screen clutter.
This UI simplification affects Free, Plus, and Pro plans and reflects the growing number of ChatGPT tools that can no longer fit as individual icons in the mobile composer.

title: Sunsetting Monday GPT
content: Launched on April 1 as a one-month surprise, our “Monday” personality (available in both voice and text) has now been sunset. We hope you enjoyed Monday's irreverent style, and we’re already working on new personalities for future use. Please stay tuned for what’s next!
title_updated: ChatGPT: Sunset Monday personality after one-month surprise run
content_updated:
OpenAI sunsets the Monday personality, an irreverent text and voice personality that launched on April 1 as a planned one-month surprise feature, with new personalities already in development.
The personality was available in both voice and text modes during its limited run, offering a distinctly different tone from ChatGPT's standard communication style.
Monday served as an experimental personality feature that preceded the broader personality system launched with GPT-5 in August 2025, with user feedback likely informing the design of subsequent personality options.

title: Updates to GPT-4o
content:
We've reverted the most recent update to GPT-4o due to issues with overly agreeable responses (sycophancy).
We’re actively working on further improvements. For more details, check out ourblog postexplaining what happened and our initial findings, andthis blog postwhere we expand on what we missed with sycophancy and the changes we're going to make going forward.
title_updated: GPT-4o: Revert update due to sycophancy issues with overly agreeable responses
content_updated:
OpenAI reverts the most recent GPT-4o update due to issues with overly agreeable responses identified as sycophancy, where the model provided excessive validation rather than honest, direct answers.
The revert restores the previous GPT-4o behavior while OpenAI works on further improvements, with detailed explanations published in blog posts about what happened and planned changes.
This incident highlighted the challenge of balancing conversational warmth with honesty in language models, leading to OpenAI's public commitment to addressing sycophancy as a systematic research priority.

title: Improvements to GPT-4o
content: We’re making additional improvements to GPT-4o, optimizing when it saves memories and enhancing problem-solving capabilities for STEM. We’ve also made subtle changes to the way it responds, making it more proactive and better at guiding conversations toward productive outcomes. We think these updates help GPT-4o feel more intuitive and effective across a variety of tasks–we hope you agree!
title_updated: GPT-4o: Improve memory optimization, STEM problem-solving & conversational guidance
content_updated:
OpenAI makes additional improvements to GPT-4o, optimizing when it saves memories, enhancing problem-solving for STEM tasks, and making it more proactive at guiding conversations toward productive outcomes.
Subtle response style changes make GPT-4o more intuitive and effective across a variety of tasks, with the model becoming better at steering interactions constructively rather than passively responding.
These updates represent the latest in a series of GPT-4o refinements, following the sycophancy revert in late April and preceding the GPT-5 launch in August 2025.

title: o3 and o4-mini in ChatGPT
content: Today, we’re releasing OpenAIo3ando4-mini,the latest in our o-series of models trained to think for longer before responding. These are the smartest models we’ve released to date, representing a step change in ChatGPT's capabilities for everyone from curious users to advanced researchers. For the first time, our reasoning models can agentically use and combine every tool within ChatGPT—this includes searching the web, analyzing uploaded files and other data with Python, reasoning deeply about visual inputs, and even generating images. Critically, these models are trained to reason about when and how to use tools to produce detailed and thoughtful answers in the right output formats, typically in under a minute, to solve more complex problems. This allows them to tackle multi-faceted questions more effectively, a step toward a more agentic ChatGPT that can independently execute tasks on your behalf. The combined power of state-of-the-art reasoning with full tool access translates into significantly stronger performance across academic benchmarks and real-world tasks, setting a new standard in both intelligence and usefulness.
title_updated: o3 & o4-mini: Launch reasoning models with full tool access in ChatGPT
content_updated:
OpenAI releases o3 and o4-mini as the latest o-series reasoning models in ChatGPT, the smartest models to date, with the first-ever ability to agentically use and combine all ChatGPT tools including web search, file analysis, Python, visual reasoning, and image generation.
The models are trained to reason about when and how to use tools, typically responding in under a minute, with significantly stronger performance across academic benchmarks and real-world tasks for multi-faceted questions.
This marks a step toward a more agentic ChatGPT that can independently execute tasks, combining state-of-the-art reasoning with full tool access to set a new standard in both intelligence and usefulness.

title: Memory with Search
content: ChatGPT can also use memories to inform search queries when ChatGPT searches the web using third-party search providers.Learn more about search.
title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Enable memory-informed web search queries
content_updated:
ChatGPT can now use saved memories to inform search queries when performing web searches through third-party search providers, making search results more relevant to the user's context and preferences.
This integration means search queries are automatically enriched with relevant personal context from memories, without requiring users to manually include background information in their prompts.
The feature builds on ChatGPT's memory system to bridge the gap between personalized conversation and web search, enabling more contextually relevant results.

title: ChatGPT Image Library
content: All images you create with ChatGPT are now automatically saved to a newLibraryin the sidebar, giving you one place to browse, revisit, and reuse your work without digging through past conversations. The Library is rolling out today on Web, iOS, and Android for Free, Plus, and Pro users (Enterprise / Edu support coming soon). For now, it displays images generated with 4o Image Generation while we backfill older creations, and you can remove an image by deleting the conversation where it was made.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch Image Library for browsing & reusing generated images
content_updated:
All images created with ChatGPT are now automatically saved to a new Library in the sidebar, providing one place to browse, revisit, and reuse generated images without searching through past conversations.
Rolling out on Web, iOS, and Android for Free, Plus, and Pro users, the Library currently displays 4o-generated images while older creations are backfilled, with deletion available by removing the source conversation.
Enterprise and Edu support is coming soon, making this one of the first ChatGPT features to launch with a dedicated content management interface outside of conversation history.

title: Sunsetting GPT‑4 in ChatGPT
content:
Effective  April  30,  2025, GPT‑4 will be retired from ChatGPT and fully replaced by GPT‑4o.
GPT‑4o is our newer, natively multimodal model. In head‑to‑head evaluations it consistently surpasses GPT‑4 in writing, coding, STEM, and more.
Recent upgrades have further improved GPT‑4o’s instruction following, problem solving, and conversational flow, making it a natural successor to GPT‑4.
GPT-4 will still be available in the API.
GPT‑4 marked a pivotal moment in ChatGPT’s evolution. We’re grateful for the breakthroughs it enabled and for the feedback that helped shape its successor. GPT‑4o builds on that foundation to deliver even greater capability, consistency, and creativity.
title_updated: GPT-4: Announce retirement from ChatGPT effective April 30, replaced by GPT-4o
content_updated:
OpenAI announces that GPT-4 will be retired from ChatGPT on April 30, 2025, with GPT-4o serving as the full replacement, while GPT-4 remains available in the API.
GPT-4o consistently surpasses GPT-4 in head-to-head evaluations across writing, coding, STEM, and more, with recent upgrades further improving instruction following, problem solving, and conversational flow.
GPT-4 marked a pivotal moment in ChatGPT's evolution, and its retirement represents the natural progression as GPT-4o builds on that foundation with greater capability, consistency, and creativity.

title: Improvements to GPT-4o
content:
We’ve made improvements to GPT-4o—it now feels more intuitive, creative, and collaborative, with enhanced instruction-following, smarter coding capabilities, and a clearer communication style.
Smarter problem-solving in STEM and coding:: GPT-4o has further improved its capability to tackle complex technical and coding problems. It now generates cleaner, simpler frontend code, more accurately thinks through existing code to identify necessary changes, and consistently produces coding outputs that successfully compile and run, streamlining your coding workflows.
Enhanced instruction-following and formatting accuracy:: GPT-4o is now more adept at following detailed instructions, especially for prompts containing multiple or complex requests. It improves on generating outputs according to the format requested and achieves higher accuracy in classification tasks.
“Fuzzy” improvements:: Early testers say that the model seems to better understand the implied intent behind their prompts, especially when it comes to creative and collaborative tasks. It’s also slightly more concise and clear, using fewer markdown hierarchies and emojis for responses that are easier to read, less cluttered, and more focused. We're curious to see if our users also find this to be the case.
“Fuzzy” improvements:: This model is now available in ChatGPT and in the API as the newest snapshot of chatgpt-4o-latest. We plan to bring these improvements to a dated model in the API in the coming weeks.
title_updated: GPT-4o: Improve creativity, coding, STEM & instruction following
content_updated:
GPT-4o receives a major update making it more intuitive, creative, and collaborative, with enhanced instruction following, smarter coding that generates cleaner frontend code, and more accurate STEM problem-solving.
The model better understands implied intent behind prompts for creative tasks, produces code that compiles and runs more reliably, follows complex multi-part instructions more accurately, and delivers slightly more concise responses with fewer markdown hierarchies and emojis.
This update is available in ChatGPT and the API as the newest chatgpt-4o-latest snapshot, with plans to bring these improvements to a dated API model in the coming weeks.

title: We have been working on a variety of usability and performance improvements and bug fixes across Web
content: We have been working on a variety of usability and performance improvements and bug fixes across Web, Android, and iOS. Below are a small number of specific callouts. For updates on the ChatGPT macOS app, please see this page.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Announce usability & performance improvements across Web, Android & iOS
content_updated:
ChatGPT announces a variety of usability and performance improvements and bug fixes across Web, Android, and iOS platforms with a small number of specific callouts.
No specific features, changes, or timeline were provided beyond the general announcement in the release note.
For updates on the ChatGPT macOS app, users are directed to a separate page, suggesting platform-specific release notes are maintained independently.

title: Web and the Windows desktop app
content:
1. In-line message error retries: If you encounter a message error, you can retry or just continue chatting in the same conversation.
2. o1 and o3-mini now offer Python-powered data analysis in ChatGPT: You can now ask these models to perform tasks like running regressions on test data, visualizing complex business metrics, and conducting scenario-based simulations.
3. Conversation Drafts: Unsubmitted messages in your message prompt will now be saved.
4. New UI for Temporary Chats: We’ve made accessing and viewing these private conversations more clear.
title_updated: ChatGPT Web & Windows: Add inline error retries, o1/o3-mini data analysis & draft saving
content_updated:
ChatGPT Web and Windows desktop app receive four updates: inline message error retries that let users retry or continue chatting, Python-powered data analysis for o1 and o3-mini models, conversation draft saving for unsubmitted messages, and a new UI for Temporary Chats.
The o1 and o3-mini data analysis enables tasks like running regressions, visualizing business metrics, and conducting scenario-based simulations directly within conversations.
These quality-of-life improvements address common friction points including lost messages after errors, limited model capabilities for data work, and disappearing draft messages.

title: Android App
content:
1. Increased the default size for in‐line generated images.
2. More clear and private Incognito keyboard for Temporary Chats.
title_updated: ChatGPT Android: Increase default image size & add incognito keyboard for Temporary Chats
content_updated:
ChatGPT Android app receives two updates: increased default size for inline generated images making them more visible, and a more clear and private incognito keyboard for Temporary Chats.
The larger image display improves the visual experience when viewing AI-generated images within conversations, while the incognito keyboard adds privacy for temporary chat sessions.
These Android-specific improvements are part of a broader cross-platform update wave covering Web, Windows, Android, and iOS released on March 18, 2025.

title: iOS App
content:
1. Improved table-copying functionality out of ChatGPT responses on iOS and macOS.
2. Added long press on your message to open menu actions: Copy, Edit.
3. Improved response display of nested blockquote content.
4. Improved display and faster streaming of conversations and message menu actions.
title_updated: ChatGPT iOS: Improve table copying, add long-press menus & faster streaming
content_updated:
ChatGPT iOS app receives four updates: improved table-copying functionality for iOS and macOS, long-press on messages to access Copy and Edit actions, improved nested blockquote display, and faster conversation streaming with message menu actions.
These improvements focus on content interaction and display quality, making it easier to extract, navigate, and read complex formatted content in chat responses.
The updates are part of a broader cross-platform release covering Web, Windows, Android, and iOS on March 18, 2025, with iOS receiving the most interaction-focused improvements.

title: GeneralGPT-4.5was released to users on the Pro plan in ChatGPT.The temporary chat icon is now in top
content:
GeneralGPT-4.5was released to users on the Pro plan in ChatGPT.The temporary chat icon is now in top bar to make the temporary chat experience more accessible and clear.
iOSLong press to selecttext on iOS and moved the message quick actions to always be visible below the message:Bug fixes and small improvements in the1.2025.056 iOS release.A setting to allow users to auto-submit transcriptions.OFF by default.
AndroidImproved error handling for Play Integrity errors.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch GPT-4.5 for Pro users & improve temporary chat accessibility
content_updated:
GPT-4.5 is released to Pro plan users in ChatGPT, and the temporary chat icon moves to the top bar for improved accessibility and clarity across the interface.
iOS receives long-press text selection, always-visible message quick actions, bug fixes in the 1.2025.056 release, and an opt-in setting for auto-submitting transcriptions. Android receives improved error handling for Play Integrity errors.
This bundled release combines a major model launch for Pro users with cross-platform UX improvements targeting everyday interaction quality.

title: 1. iOS widget (see below):
content:
1. iOS widget (see below):
2. Text selection improvements: you can now select text across horizontal rulers.3. Performance improvements: Improved model header loading behavior. Conversation history and GPTs show up faster.4. Bug fix for logged-out users: 401s when dismissing tooltips  401s logging out users on Android.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch iOS widget, text selection fixes & performance improvements
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces an iOS widget alongside text selection improvements that now support selection across horizontal rulers and performance improvements for faster model header loading and conversation history display.
A bug fix resolves 401 errors that were logging out users on Android when dismissing tooltips, and GPT display shows up faster across the interface.
This release focuses on platform-specific quality improvements, with the iOS widget being the headline feature for quick ChatGPT access from the home screen.

title: Set ChatGPT as default search engine in Safari.
content:
Set ChatGPT as default search engine in Safari.
Confirmation dialog for logging out (iOS, web).
Improved Android conversation parsing performance.
iOS performance fixes.
Updated launch animations and composer design on Android.
Whisper (ChatGPT's voice-to-text feature) now shows a text preview of dictated text after recording:
title_updated: ChatGPT: Add Safari search engine option, logout confirmation & dictation preview
content_updated:
ChatGPT can now be set as the default search engine in Safari, and a new confirmation dialog prevents accidental logouts on iOS and web.
Additional updates include improved Android conversation parsing performance, iOS performance fixes, updated Android launch animations and composer design, and a voice-to-text dictation preview showing transcribed text after recording.
The Safari search engine integration marks ChatGPT's entry into the browser search market, competing directly with traditional search engines for default search position.

title: Canvas sharing
content:
Users can now share a Canvas asset such as rendered React/HTML code, document, or code with another user, similar to how you share a conversation. You can do this from the Canvas toolbar when Canvas is open.
For more details about the canvas feature in ChatGPT, check out the following Help Center article!
title_updated: ChatGPT Canvas: Launch sharing for rendered code, documents & code assets
content_updated:
ChatGPT Canvas now supports sharing assets including rendered React and HTML code, documents, and code with other users, similar to existing conversation sharing functionality.
Sharing is accessible from the Canvas toolbar when Canvas is open, enabling direct collaboration on Canvas-created outputs without requiring recipients to recreate the content.
This extends Canvas from a personal creation tool into a collaborative platform where users can share interactive code previews, formatted documents, and code snippets with others.

title: More personalization in Custom Instructions
content:
We've updated custom instructions to make it easier to customize how ChatGPT responds to you. With the new UI, you can tell ChatGPT the traits you want it to have, how you want it to talk to you, and any rules you want it to follow.
If you're already using custom instructions, this won't change your current settings.
The new UI is rolling out now onhttp://chatgpt.comand desktop on Windows, and is coming to mobile and desktop on MacOS in the next few weeks. Available soon to ChatGPT users in the EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Switzerland.
title_updated: ChatGPT: Update custom instructions UI with traits, communication style & rules
content_updated:
ChatGPT updates custom instructions with a new UI that makes it easier to specify desired traits, communication style, and rules for ChatGPT to follow, without changing existing custom instruction settings.
The new UI is rolling out on chatgpt.com and Windows desktop first, with mobile and macOS support coming in the following weeks, and will be available soon for EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Switzerland users.
This redesign makes personalization more structured and accessible compared to the previous freeform text approach, guiding users to specify preferences across distinct categories.

title: Import from Chat into Canvas
content:
Edit a full model response in Canvas
Edit a code block in Canvas
Web only (no desktop or mobile in this release).
title_updated: ChatGPT Canvas: Launch import from chat for editing responses & code blocks
content_updated:
ChatGPT Canvas now lets users import and edit full model responses or individual code blocks from chat directly into the Canvas editor, available on web only.
This feature enables users to take any ChatGPT response and move it into Canvas for in-place editing, refinement, and collaboration without manual copy-pasting.
Desktop and mobile support are not included in this initial release, with Canvas import functionality being web-exclusive as the feature launches.

title: Scheduled tasks in ChatGPT
content:
Today we’re rolling out a beta version of tasks—a new way to ask ChatGPT to do things for you at a future time. Whether it's one-time reminders or recurring actions, tell ChatGPT what you need and when, and it will automatically take care of it.
Scheduled tasks is in early beta for Plus, Pro, and Teams. Eventually this will be available to anyone with a ChatGPT account.
For details see Scheduled tasks in ChatGPT.
title_updated: ChatGPT Tasks: Launch scheduled automated prompts in beta for Plus, Pro & Teams
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces Tasks in early beta, a new capability for scheduling one-time reminders or recurring automated prompts that ChatGPT executes at specified future times.
The feature is available for Plus, Pro, and Teams plans, with plans to eventually make it available to anyone with a ChatGPT account.
Scheduled Tasks represents ChatGPT's first asynchronous automation feature, moving beyond real-time conversation toward proactive task execution on the user's behalf.
